Dance Central - Whisk
Samba Whisk
The Samba Whisk is Newcomer Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
There are 2 styles of Whisks: 
Whisk Style 1 - bigger figure 8 hip movement
Leader
Closed Position, use bounce action.
Alignments: Facing Wall, or other alignments can be used.
1: LF to side | BF 
Whisk to Left
a: RF behind LF w/o weight (cuban cross) | T (pressure)
2: take minimal weight to RF and replace weight to LF | BF
1: RF to side | BF
Whisk to Right
a: LF behind RF w/o weight (cuban cross) | T (pressure)
2: take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF | BF
Follower
1: RF to side | BF
Should end "1" on counter promenade position (body turns less, hip turns 1/8 more).
Rib cage is to the right, L hip left behind, lifted, squeeze L side, L hip up high, and L leg is elongated to create long leg line. 
Always hold on "1" for 3/4 beat. until the "a" count to move LF. Do not release LF early.
a: LF behind RF w/o weight (cuban cross) | T (pressure)
Hip continue to do figure 8: send hip to right and forward and then back
LF to cuban cross position behind RF, do not step too big, L hip figure 8 action
Alternatively, lady can do underarm turn to R. See note below.
2: take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF | BF
at end of "2", flexed R knee; keep legs tight; hip finish one figure 8. and start moving the opposite direction.
1: LF to side | BF
Alternatively, lady can do underarm turn to L, which is a slower turn than turning to R (Lady is already in CPP.) See note below.
a: RF behind LF w/o weight (cuban cross) | T (pressure)
2: take minimal weight to RF and replace weight to LF | BF
Whisk Style 2 - bigger bounce action
Using Lady's steps as example: 
1: RF to side | BF
Starting position: cuban cross position (RF cross behind LF), both knees bent, L hip higher.
From previous beat "&": 
"&a": Prepare: start rising, straightening knee, while rotating L hip to L (more to L side, do not over rotate to back, hips are in more neutral position at this time),
The action makes R leg goes to side, straight R leg, no weight (weight is still on LF), both heels are up. Do not stick butt out back. Keep toes on floor (not in the air during the movement.)
1", weight switch to RF, compress tummy, both knees bent forward, knees more forward and free. bigger bounce action.
Neck/shoulder are free, relaxed, not held stiff
Next "&: start rising
a: LF behind RF w/o weight (cuban cross) | T (pressure)
LF cuban cross behind RF, do not step too big, do not step too big. or too far behind, both heels up, L hip figure 8 action, finish L hip to back.
2: take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF | BF
at end of "2", flex R knee; heels lower, hip finish one figure 8. and start moving the opposite direction.
1: LF to side | BF
a: RF behind LF w/o weight (cuban cross) | T (pressure)
2: take minimal weight to RF and replace weight to LF | BF
Notes:
Whisk (in all dances) is used to change position. So you want to go to counter promenade position on one side, and promenade position on the other side.
Lady's Underarm Turn to R: While Man does whisk to L, lady can do underarm turn to R: use the rise of the first step to make the turn:
slow turn: "and": turn 1/4 to R, "a 2": turn 3/4 to R
sharp turn:
Focus head/eyes at Man, spot, turn shoulder to R first, then sharp turn, leg follow. Head is straight up (don't do shape as in Standard).
RF almost stays same place. Ending RF cross in front of LF, knees tucked.
By "a", should already finish the turn, face partner, do not under turn.
Lady's Underarm Turn to L: While Man does whisk to R, Lady can do underarm turn to L when stepping LF to side.
When Man raises the arm, start turning to L, shape upper body to L and back, stretch R side, head looking to left, turn 1/4 to L, LF is a fwd step.
Keep turning, RF press floor (after turning another 1/4 L, RF fwd, away from Man, then continue 1/2 to L to turn to face partner), regain normal shaping. Slide LF cross in front of RF, knees tucked.

Cha Cha Cha Reverse Top
Cha Cha Reverse Top is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
2: RF to side and slightly fwd | Up to three complete turns over 1-15
Man's L toe remains in same place.
3: Swivel on ball of LF, ending LF in front of RF (Cuban Cross)
Maintain frame all the time and keep LF in about same place.
4: RF to side and slightly fwd
&1 234&1 2 4&1 Repeat 2 & 3 six times
Follower
2: LF behind RF (Cuban Cross) | Up to three complete turns over 1-15
The turning action is continuous.
Body keeps turning, and then stepping. Foot keeps turning.
Knees will not straighten fully until last step 15.
Only slight natural hip movement is used.
3: RF back and slightly side
Lady rotates around Man's L toe which remains in place.
Lady: maintain shoulders, body center and hipos facing Man.
4 & 1: LF back, toe turned out, to chasse LRL
2: RF back and slightly to side
3: LF behind RF (Cuban Cross)
4 & 1: RF back and slightly to side to chasse RLR
2 3 4& 1 Repeat steps 1-5

Dance Central - Spiral
Cha Cha Cha Spiral
Cha Cha Spiral is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
A spiral turn is danced by Lady and is a turn made on RF turning L or on LF turning R, allowing the other foot to cross in front without weight. Normally a spiral turn will end with ankles crossed although the cross in the Curl is looser.
Spiral commences in Closed Position.
Leader
2: LF to side or fwd in RSP | slight body turn to R
Steps are very similar to that of Closed Hip Twist. Increase tone and lower L arm to R at chest level. Widen R arm in hold to R side.
3: Transfer weight to RF
Keep tone in arms
4 & 1: Compact Chasse LRL | very slight body turn to L
Return arms to normal position over 3 & 4. Raise L hand above Lady's R shoulder to circle counterclockwise on step 5. If preferred, Man can keep his fingers lightly in contact with Lady's waist as Lady does her spiral turn.
2: RF back | slight body turn to L, to normal position
L arm to L side lowering L hand to waist level.
3: Transfer weight to LF | 1/8 to L over 7-10
Increase tone in L arm.
4 & 1: RF to side to chasse RLR, end in Fan Position
Slightly extend L arm. L hand still. (Man can do a slip twist chasse.)
Follower
2: RF back in RSP | 1/2 to R
Turn is commenced at end of previous beat.
3: Transfer weight to LF
4 & 1: RF fwd and slightly across LF in front of Man to chasse RLR, then turn underarm, allowing LF to cross in front without weight (Spiral turn) | 1/4 to L between 2 &3. Then a further 7/8 on step 5 doing spiral.
Count 1: 
Lady may use an Extended Forward Walk.
Spiral: turn is made on ball of RF with foot flat (T of LF). Turn could also be made on R toe with heel well off the floor.
See Note on Spiral Turn.
2: LF fwd | 1/8 to L
3: RF fwd and slightly across, then turn to end RF back | 1/8 to L, then a further 1/2 to L. Body turns less.
4 & 1: Back lock LRL. End in Fan Position | Body completes turn
Can also finish in other positions, see Note below.
Notes
Spiral Turn:
Put full weight on the spiral foot (in this case, RF), with head, center, hips all stacked up. Do not leave hip behind. Put whole weight on foot as fast as possible before doing spiral.
Turning, Man lead arm, move elbow across body, do not turn and leave arm and elbow behind.
As spiraling, keep thighs crossed, helps to pull legs together fast.
Undertuned spiral - an alternative:
Man: turn 1/8 L over 7-10, dancing a fwd lock to end in Open Position.
Lady: 
3/4 L after placing step 5, to end in Tandem Position.
Step 6: LF fwd
Step 7: RF fwd in line with LF, then continue to turn 1/2 to end with RF back.
Step 8-10: Back lock LRL.
Alternative finishing positions
Open Position: Man turns 3/8 to L over steps 7-10.
Open CPP
Contact Position: ( Known as Closed Spiral)
Man turn 5/8 to L over steps 7-10, dancing the chasse side and slightly fwd, overturning Lady, keeping fingers lightly in contact with Lady's waist throughout as Lady turns within the circle of his R arm. On 6: lower arms to normal position. Man keeps his RH in contact with Lady's back to indicate finishing in contact position
Lady makes a complete turn L over steps 6-10, dancing the chasse with LF behind RF as Cuban Cross. 
Left Side Position
Man: no turn over 7 to 10, leading Lady to overturn on 7, lowering L arm and taking it slightly fwd.
Lady: turn an additional 1/8 to L on 7, using a loosely crossed spiral action
Both dance a fwd lock on steps 8-10.

Spot Turns, Switch Turns, Underarm Turns
Cha Cha Cha Switch Turns
The Cha Cha Switch Turns are Bronze Level figures. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
2: RF fwd (after turning 1/4 to L on preceding "&") in line with LF ,Then turn 1/2 to end with RF in the back on "&" | 1/4 to L, step RF, then 1/2 to L
This is Switch Turn to Left.
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
3: Transfer weight to LF
"3&", on "&": turn 1/4 to L
4 & 1: RF to side to chasse RLR | 1/4 to L over 3-5
At end of "1": turn to R.
2: LF fwd (after 1/4 to R) in line with RF, then turn 1/2 to end with LF in the back | 1/4 to R, step LF, then 1/2 to R
This is Switch Turn to Right.
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
3: Transfer weight to RF, 
on "&", turn 1/4 to R
4 & 1: LF to side to chasse LRL | 1/4 to R over 3-5
Follower
2: LF fwd (after turning 1/4 to R on preceding "&") in line with RF. Then turn 1/2 to R to end with LF in the back on "&" | 1/4 to R, step LF, Then 1/2 to R on "&"
This is Switch Turn to Left (because Man is turning L)
End of preceding step on '&', fully transfer weight, (tone in arms, feeling like pressing into Man's arm), turn 1/4 to R, swivel RF and then strike LF fwd, LF turned out. Pay attention to foot position, not turned in.
Different from New Yorker: For this step, commit weight on front foot before doing the half turn to R. See Forward Walk Turn technique.
Arms: Lady turning to R, L arm behind, R arm in front, R palm facing tummy, wrapping around body while turning. 
3: Transfer weight on to RF
Coaches do it differently:
Option: before "3", there is a moment the R leg is bent, then straighten before stepping on floor.
Option: Keep R leg straight the whole time.
On "&", fully transfer weight, turn 1/4 to R (turn before stepping next step). See Swivel technique.
One Variation is that on "&", body continue turning to maximum, then quick leg movement in 4&1.

Cha Cha Cha Spot Turns
Spot Turn is technically defined as "two forward steps and a Chasse to side danced solo by Man and/or Lady circling either to L or R".
Partner may dance Time Step, or Closed Basic. Or, Man and Lady can dance this together.
Variations:
A complete turn is made when Man and Lady start and end facing each other.
7/8 turn is made when starting in Open PP or Open CPP and ending facing each other, or vice versa; or when starting in LSP or RSP and ending facing partner.
3/4 turn is made when starting in Open PP and ending in Open CPP or vice verse.
Spot Turns are popular ending to New York, Hand to Hand, etc. and can be danced as Switch Turns.
To execute Spot Turns "by the book":
"2": LF diagonally fwd and across to Man's Left side, shoulder is parallel to Man (common mistake is to turn everything). Use CBMP to create torque. Use Forward Walk Turn action on "&" to do 1/2 turn. Finish with "tail bone" down. 2 variations for RF:
brush action: as we do rotation, close and collect on "&"
switch action: no collection on "&", just turn and replace weight on "3".
"3": Get weight onto RF foot, do Swivel action to outside to turn and face partner. Do not rush to turn to face partner.
Don't skip over middle positions. Make sure weight is fully shifted to front of foot before swivel.
Pay special attention: the hip needs to have a connected movement with spine, so the hip shifts forward when spine moves forward. Head should not stick forward, butt back. There is no break at hip. Gets hip, rib cage, head on one vertical line.
Finish with tail bone down. so there is a good axis for swivel.
Don't try to turn early. Finish one step first before next step.
As right side pull through to swivel, inside edge of left foot tracks underneath. Track seamlessly, not pausing. (Advanced dancers can choose to pause for accent - conscious decision. For basic actions, just track smoothly through.)
"4&1": LF side chasse.
Connecting Figures are the same as Switch Turns.
Cha Cha Cha Underarm Turns
Underarm Turn to Left (Lady's Left)
This can start in Closed Position or Open CPP. L to R hand hold.
From Closed Position:
Man does 1-5 step of Closed Basic, while leading Lady to do a Switch Turn turning to her L by raising his L arm and circling counter clockwise.
From Open CPP:
Man does 1-5 step of Closed Basic, step 1 will be towards Lady's L side and turning 1/8 L over 3-5, then lead as above.
Man may turn 1/8 L on last step to end in Open PP (Lady turns 1/8 less).
Underarm Turn to Right (Lady's Right)
This starts in Closed Position. L to R hand hold.
From Closed Position:
Man does 6-10 step of Closed Basic, while leading Lady to do Switch Turn, turning to her R, by raising his L arm and circling clockwise.
Step 6: heel needs to be on the ground, straight back, not turn (which hints Alemena).
Man may turn 1/8 to R to end in Open CPP. Lady turns 1/8 Less.

Dance Central - Time Step
Cha Cha Cha Time Step
The Cha Cha Time Step is a Newcomer Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Time Steps can be danced any time in Closed or Open Position without hold, or having released hold at end of previous figure.
Left Foot Time Step
2: LF behind RF (Cuban Cross)
Feet are in 3rd position. R knee bent (not straight leg as in other patterns)
3: Replace weight to RF
4 & 1: LF to side to chasse LRL
Right Foot Time Step
2: RF behind LF (Cuban Cross)
Feet are in 3rd position, Latin Cross position, really wrap twist hip around the core to the right, while maintaining straight body line.
Option 1: L knee bent (not straight leg as in other patterns)
Option 2: L leg straight.
3: Replace weight to LF
4 & 1: RF to side to chasse RLR
Note:
There is no physical lead, typical connecting figures:
Man dances a Spot or Switch Turn, or LF Cuban Break, while Lady dances RF Time Step.
Man dances a RF time step while Lady does Spot or Switch Turn, or LF Cuban Break.

Normal Hold

Leader
When Leader is doing Three Cha Cha Chas Forward, Follower is doing Three Cha Cha Chas Back.
2: RF back

At end of "3": twist
4: RF latin cross in front LF
Side lead, start Three Cha Cha Chas Forward
&: LF locks behind RF
LF knee to the spot. Then, release R knee, L knee straight, step RF into next step.
1: RF steps directly under R shoulder, on center line
2 separate tracks; at the end, twist to R, side lead

2 & 3: LF Latin Cross; RF lock, LF forward

4 & 1: RF Latin Cross; LF lock, RF forward

2: LF forward check

&: RF locks behind LF
1: LF forward | Turn to R

Dance Central - Samba Technique
Samba Timing
Samba has various combinations of timings and beat values. The chart below also shows different methods of counting.
Notation: Break 1 beat into 4 quarters beat (1234), it can be noted as (1 2 3 4) or (1 e & a). "&" is half beat, "e" and "a" are 1/8 and 3/4 beat. 1 bar of music is 2 beats (1234 5678). 
Rhythm
Count | # of steps | # of beats (1 bar = 2 beats) | Beat Value | Alternate method of counting | Breaking 1 beat into quarter note | Examples
1 a 2 | 3 steps | 2 beats | 3/4 1/4 1 | S a S | (123) 4 (5678)
Basic Movements Whisk Boto Fogas
1 a 2 a 3 a 4 | 7 steps | 4 beats | 3/4 1/4 3/4 1/4 3/4 1/4 1 | S a S a S a S | (123) 4 (567) 8 (123) 4 (5678)
Volta
1 2 3 | 3 steps | 2 beats | 3/4 1/2 3/4 | (123) (45) (678)
Promenade Runs
SQQ | 3 steps | 2 beats | 1 1/2 1/2 | 1. 2 and | (1234) (56) (78)
Natural Roll Reverse Roll
SQQQQQQ | 7 steps | 4 beats | 1 1/2 1/2 1/2 1/2 1/2 1/2 | 1. 2 and 3 and 4 and | (1234) (56) (78) (12) (34) (56) (78)
Corta Jaca
QQS | 3 steps | 2 beats | 1/2 1/2 1 | 1 and 2 | (12) (34) (5678)
Samba Locks
SSQQS | 5 steps | 4 beats | 1 1 1/2 1/2 1 | 1. 2. 3 and 4 | (1234) (5678) (12) (34) (5678)
Cruzado Walks and Locks
1 2 | 2 steps | 2 beats | 1 1 | SS | (1234) (5678)
Samba Actions
Keep good compact frame. Chest more open, uplifting feeling. 
Samba Bounce and Foot Movement Rhythm
Samba Bounce
The characteristic movement of Samba is the Samba Bounce. Bounce action happens on whole beat and "&" count. 
Overall posture:
Head stays fairly flat, do not pop up and down.
Chest lifted (so lower body free to move), shoulder down, neck long. Upper body is quiet, but not tense/stiff.
Soft knees, body weight more forward on front of foot.
1: First 1/2 of the beat - going "down" (which is really a knee forward action): 
Compress/contract tummy, pelvic pendulum forward action, tail bone down, underneath body. 
It's important to connect the pelvis to thigh. Imagine standing in front of a wall with some space in between. When bouncing forward, it's not knee touching the wall, it is thigh touching the wall. 
Flex the knee, think about knee going "forward", knee slightly pass the feet. (do not think about "down" action, which makes it look heavy.)
Absorb the bounce in body, head height stays about the same, should not have very big up and down changes
Do not crunch shoulder forward to try to achieve the look. Shoulder/neck relaxed, calm.
&: Second 1/2 of the beat - going up: 
"Release": Contract back muscle (shorten the spine), pelvic pendulum back action. Slight straightening of the knees. Knees are not locked. 
Back is normal position, do not try to "stick butt out" too much.
Whole beat and half beat are exact point when body changes direction
Bounce is even, smooth, subtle and not exaggerated. Soft knees. 
Height does not change much. If you keep pelvis and straightening knee only, then the head goes up and down. Manage the height level with pelvis. 
Knee straighten, straight leg, pelvis back.
Knee forward, bent leg, pelvis under/fwd.
Even thought it's "bounce", but think more about connecting pelvis to thighs, moving "forward and back", tail bone going forward and back, keeping same height. 
Two beats to a bar in Samba music, 2 complete bounce actions to one bar.
Bounce action is used on all figures having a count of "1a2 3a4" basic timing and Volta timing (1a2a3a4).
Bounce is not used where this is a Q in timing, or 1 2 3. (exception: Cruzado Walk and Locks).
Many Samba figures combines with figure 8 actions and bounce action.
When practice:
Stand on one foot, e.g. LF, whole foot flat. RF to side, two feet are not too wide apart (which will limit bounce action), inside edge of ball of RF, bent R knee, R heel up, Keep left heel down during bounce.
1&2&3&4 - switch weight to the other foot - 1&2&3&4
Foot Movement
(This applies to Samba Basic and Volta timing.)
One of the complexities in Samba is that foot moves at a different time from the bounce action. Foot moves/places (no weight, just pressure) on quarter note "a", while body bounces on whole and "&" count.
Putting It Together
Starting with weight on LF, RF will step forward, using Natural Basic as an example, chart below shows the bounce and foot actions over 1 bar of music (2 beats). Each beat is broken into quarter beat.
Bounce (body changes direction) happens on count 1, 3, 5, 7 (whole and half beat)
Foot moves (without weight) on quarter note: count 4 and 8.
Samba Natural Basic: (Quarter Beat # | Count | Bounce Action | Foot Action)
7 | & | Body rising up, strong | -
This is starting second half of the preceding beat.
Start to straighten knees.
The straightening comes from pushing the balls of feet off the floor. (Do not do this mechanically from body. It needs to come from the foot.)
8 | a | Body continue to rise | RF places fwd, no weight, just pressure
Shorten the back, "Samba Pulse", Pressure through ball of LF, move RF fwd with slight pressure on RF, but no weight. Pelvic pendulum back.
Weight is still on LF. Think about opposition. Step underneath the body.
Do not move spine over too quickly.
1 | 1 | Body reaches peak rise, and change direction to go down (knee forward), slight acceleration. | -
Take weight onto RF. Slightly flex the knees before lowering the heel, then the knees will continue to flex slightly after heel has lowered.
Contract ab muscle, Pelvic pendulum forward action.
Free leg that's behind: the foot should be IE, roll the ankle downward, point back, create beautiful leg line. Free leg position is very important.
2 | e | Body continue to go down | -
Knees continue to flex after heel has lowered. Think about ice cream scoop action.
3 | & | Body reaches bottom and change direction to rise up, slight acceleration | -
Technique is the same as above. Start straightening knees (coming from ball of RF).
4 | a | Body continue to rise | LF places fwd, 3rd position, pressure only, no weight
Slightly straighten knee, draw LF towards RF without weight, but with pressure on ball of LF
5 | 2 | Body changes direction, going down | -
Continue to slightly straighten the knees, take minimal weight to LF, replace weight to RF on ball of foot;
Then, change direction, slightly flexing the knees and lowering heel.
6 | e | Body continue to go down | -
7 | & | Body changes direction to go up | -
8 | a | Body continue to go up | LF places back, no weight
LF is placing back, but body is still forward.
1 | 3 | Body change direction to go down | -

General Technique
See Latin Technique for various dance positions.
Last step of a figure should reflect the technique of the next figure. E.g. when going to Corta Jacas from Stationary Samba Walks, last step of stationary samba walk is on a straight leg.

Bota Fogos to Promenade and Counter Promenade
Samba Bota Fogos to Promenade and Counter Promenade
The Samba Bota Fogos to Promenade and Counter Promenade is Bronze Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Start in Closed Position. Use bounce action.
1: LF fwd | No turn | BF
a: RF to side w/o weight | 1/8 to L over 2 & 3 | I/E of T (pressure)
2: Take minimal weight to RF and replace weight to LF in PP | BF
Turn Lady to PP with pressure through RH
1: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | No turn | BF
a: LF to sdie w/o weight | 1/4 to R over 5 & 6 | I/E of T(Pressure)
Turn Lady to L with pressure through RH
2: Take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF in CPP | BF
Continue to turn Lady to CPP
1: LF fwd and across in CPP and CBMP | No turn | BF
a: RF to side w/o weight | 3/8 to L over 8 & 9. Body turns slightly less | I/E of T (pressure)
Turn Lady to R with pressure through RH
2: Take minimal weight to RF and replace weight to LF in PP | BF
Continue to turn Lady to PP
Follower
1: RF back | No turn | BF
Steps 1-3: get into Promenade Position.
a: LF to side w/o weight | 1/8 to R over 2 & 3 | I/E of T (pressure)
Lady turns R on this step to PP 
2: Take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF in PP | BF
1: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | No turn | BF
LF fwd and across RF in front.
a: RF to side w/o weight | 1/4 to L over 5 & 6 | I/E of T(Pressure)
2: Take minimal weight to RF and replace weight to LF in CPP | BF
1: RF fwd and across in CPP and CBMP | No turn | BF
RF fwd and across LF in front.
a: LF to side w/o weight | 3/8 to R over 8 & 9. Body turns slightly less | I/E of T (pressure)
2: Take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF in PP | BF
Note:
It's ok to dance only step 1-3 as Bota Fogo to PP.

Dance Central - Back Rocks
Samba Back Rocks
The Samba Back Rocks is Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
This figure starts in Closed Position.
Use "Polka Bounce": Each step is taken with a strong pressure through ball of foot, lightly lowering heel on the second half of step. Pelvis swings naturally towards the stepping foot on step 1 and 2 and start to return to normal position on step 3 (repeat for steps 4 to 6).
Leader
S: RF back | 1/8 to L | BF | 
Q: Replace weight to LF, having moved it slightly leftwards | 1/8 to L between steps 1 and 2 | BF
Man extends hold slightly to allow Lady to place full weight on to her back foot.
Q: Replace weight to RF | No turn | BF
S: LF back | 1/8 to R between 3 and 4 | BF
Q: Replace weight to RF, having moved it slightly rightwards | 1/8 to R between 4 and 5 | BF
Man extends hold slightly to allow Lady to place full weight on to her back foot.
Q: Replace weight to LF | No turn | BF
Follower
S: LF fwd | 1/8 to L | BF
Needs full weight on front of the foot, not weight in middle of two feet. Center lifted.
Really come toward close to partner, almost chest to chest.
Q: Replace weight to RF, having moved it slightly rightwards | 1/8 to L between 1 and 2 | BF
When rocking back, keep upper body more forward, think about only sending hip back to flip the skirt. Rebound elastic look.
Bring chest center in, almost a small body ripple, then hip back, so the body is not one block, looking stiff.
Heel only slightly touch the floor.
Q: Replace weight to LF | No Turn | BF
At end of beat, swivel on LF while RF is trailing through next to LF, brushes and then steps fwd.
S: RF fwd | 1/8 to R between 4 and 5 | BF
Rotate shoulder first, at the end, turn head sharply to R. Head is delayed to give it more emphasis on sharpness. Soft hips, sharp head.
Don't over swivel the back foot, back foot should still be in a turned out position.
Q: Replace weight to LF, having moved it slightly leftwards | 1/8 to R between 5 and 6 | BF
Q: Replace weight to RF | No Turn | BF
If continuing, do the swivel on RF, LF trails and draw curve on ground before stepping fwd. Delayed head turn to L on next S.
Note
This may be repeated.

Dance Central - Closed Rocks
Samba Closed Rocks
The Samba Closed Rocks is Bronze Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Start in Closed Position. No bounce. Start facing LOD.
S: RF fwd | No turn | BF
Q: LF fwd, small step | -- | BF
Q: Replace weight to RF, toe slightly turned out | -- | B
If preferred, back foot may be drawn slightly towards front foot
S: LF fwd | -- | BF
Q: RF fwd, small step | -- | BF
Q: Replace weight to LF, toe slightly turned out | -- | B
If preferred, back foot may be drawn slightly towards front foot
Step 1-3 or 1-6 may be repeated.
Follower
S: LF back | No turn | BF
Back step because Man is traveling fwd. 
Lady responds to Man's increasing tone in arms with matching tone in her arms.
Q: RF back, small step, toe turned out. Knee slightly flexed | 1/8 to R (RF) | B
The turn of foot is reflected in hips, the upper body remains square to Man. The toe of back foot is opposite heel of front foot.
Q: Replace weight to LF | No turn | BF
If preferred, front foot may be drawn slightly towards back foot
S: RF back | 1/8 to L (RF) | BF
Option: flick RF before stepping RF back - personal preference.
Q: LF back, small step, toe turned out. Knee slightly flexed. | 1/8 to L (LF) | B
Same technique as step 2.
Q: Replace weight to RF | No turn | BF
If preferred, front foot may be drawn slightly towards back foot.
Step 1-3 or 1-6 may be repeated.

Dance Central - Contra Bota Fogos
Samba Contra Bota Fogos
The Samba Contra Bota Fogos is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
This figures starts in R Contra Position. Use Bounce Action.
Leader
1: RF fwd towards Lady's R side in R Contra Position | No turn | BF
Slight pressure with R hand to lead Lady fwd
a: LF back and slightly to side without weight. Toe turned out. | 1/4 to R over steps 2, 3 | I/E of T (Pressure)
Slightly extend hold over steps 2 and 3.
2: Take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF in L Contra Position, having moved it slightly back | No turn | BF
1: LF fwd towards Lady's L side in L Contra Position | Slight pressure with R hand to lead Lady fwd, returning arms to normal position 
a: RF back and slightly to side without weight. Toe turned out | 1/4 to L over steps 5, 6
Slightly extend hold over steps 5 and 6
2: Take minimal weight to RF and replace weight to LF in R Contra Position, having moved it slightly back.
Figure may be repeated.
Follower
1: RF fwd towards Man's R side in R Contra Position | No turn | BF
a: LF back and slightly to side without weight. Toe turned out | 1/4 to R over steps 2 and 3 | I/E of T (Pressure)
2: Take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF in L Contra Position, having moved it slightly back | BF
1: LF fwd towards Man's L side in L Contra Position | No turn | BF
a: RF back and slightly to side without weight. Toe turned out. | 1/4 to L over steps 5 and 6 | I/E of T (Pressure)
2: Take minimal weight to RF and replace weight to LF in R Contra Position, having moved it slightly back | BF
Notes:
Contra Bota Fogos may be danced with hand changes as: (when danced as this, the figure is usually repeated, regain normal hold on last step.)
Start with R to R hand hold (achieved at end of previous figure)
Change to L to L hand hold on step 3
Change to R to R hand hold on step 6.

2: Take minimal weight to LF and replace weight to RF | BF
When used as a Precede to Traveling Bota Fogos Forward, start Natural Basic Movement facing LOD and turn 1/8 L over 2 and 3. 4-6 are then danced with follower outside.
When used as a Follow to Traveling Bota Fogos Forward, the first step is outside partner. Turn 1/8 R over 2 and 3 to face partner.

Follows: any figure commenced with RF.

Samba Side Basic Movement
This may be danced in place of the Natural or Reverse Basic taking the first step to side. All others are the same.
When taking side step to R side, frame to right, R knee bent, but leaving L hip to L side till the next "and", really stretch the body long without tilting shoulder; roll the hip to R (clock-wise). 2 rounds ending stepping to L.
When stepping to side, the free foot position is important: roll the foot down, IE of foot touch floor, not ball of foot.
Similarly, when taking step to L side, leave R hip to right till the next "and", roll the hip to L (counter clock-wise).

Dance Central - Argentine Crosses
Samba Argentine Crosses
The Samba Argentine Crosses is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, and movement, technique; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
This figure starts in Closed Position. No Bounce.
Leader
Q: LF fwd, toe turned out | start to turn R | BF
Normally 1 turn. But up to 2 complete turns can be made over 12 steps.
Included body slightly L over steps 1-3, 7-9 and R over 4-6 and 10-12.
Q: RF behind LF (Cuban Cross) | 1/8 to R btwn 1 and 2 | B
S: LF to side and slightly fwd | 1/8 to R btwn 2 and 3 | BF
Q: RF fwd in line with LF, small step. Toe turned out | 1/4 to R btwn 3 and 4 | BF
Q: LF to side and slightly back | 1/8 to R btwn 4 and 5 | B
S: RF fwd in line with LF, small step. Toe turned out | 1/8 to R btwn 5 and 6 | BF
QQS QQS: Repeat steps 1-6 
Follower
Q: RF fwd in line with LF, small step. Toe turned out | start to turn R | BF
Incline (shape) to R
Keep solid frame with Man, energy up, shoulder parallel to Man.
RF crosses in front once for steps 1-3, then crosses in back for steps 4-6 (if it's not underarm turn to L)
Q: LF to side and slightly back | 1/8 to R btwn 1 and 2 | B
S: RF fwd in line with LF, small step. Toe turned out. | 1/8 to R btwn 2 and 3 | BF
Q: LF fwd, toe turned out | 1/4 to R btwn 3 and 4 | BF
Incline (shape) to L 
Variation: over steps 4 to 6, Man can turn Lady to R under raised L arm and then regain hold. Lady does 1 complete turn to R after closing her left side to Man.
Q: RF behind LF (Cuban Cross) | 1/8 to R btwn 4 and 5 | B
S: LF to side and slightly fwd | 1/8 to R btwn 5 and 6 | BF
QQS QQS : Repeat steps 1-6 
Lady can do underarm turn over steps 10-12
Note:
Keep good frame with partner (more parallel)

Preceding steps: Alemana, Rope Spinning, Three Alemanas, Sliding Doors.
On last step, lead Lady towards R side then turn her R to R Shadow Position, get into one of the 3 R Shadow Holds.
Lady LF fwd in line with RF, then turn 1/2 to R on LF to end LF back.
count "2": Man and Lady then step back (Man LF, Lady RF)
count "3": Transfer weight fwd to RF (Lady LF)
count "4 1": LF fwd (Lady RF)
Follow with 6 Progressive Wlaks Forward with no turn or curving up to 1/2 to L. (Man start with RF, Lady LF)
Or dance Cuban Rock from Aida, then turn 1/2 (Man L, Lady R), taking R Shadow Position and hold, continue with 6 progressive Walks Forward as above.

count "4 1": Man could lead Lady to dance a spiral turn L when he has danced a Walk Forward on his LF (Lady RF fwd then make a complete turn to L on RF)
Continue with Progressive Walks Forward (Man starts RF, Lady LF)
Ending:
Fan Position: Man leading lady to pass in front of him to his L side, Man turns 1/8 L, Lady 7/8 L. When using Shadow hold 1, change to L to R hand hold on step 5 of Fan.
Ending facing partner: omit 6th Forward Walk and both step to side, turning 1/4 to R, Lady to L to face partner.
Spiral Ending: Omit 6th Forward Walk and close LF to RF leading Lady into a spiral turn to L.
Lady turn 1/4 L on the 6th walk to end in front of Man to dance her spiral turn.
Continue with 4-6 Spiral - this is solo spiral for Lady when R Shadow Hold 1 is used.

Dance Central - Rope Spinning
Rumba Rope Spinning
Rope Spinning is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Rope Spinning is commenced during the last step of the preceding figure. Man leads Lady to turn sharply R under raised L arm, inclining and turning his body slightly R, releasing R hand hold. Lady does Spiral, a complete turn to R on LF with foot flat (T of RF), body turning less, ending on Man's side facing opposite way. That's the Spiral Turn part. Man can keep his hand lightly in contact with Lady's back. What shows below is what follows the Spiral Turn.
Leader
2: LF to side | continue to turn body to R
Circle L hand clockwise over head over step 1 and 2
3: Transfer weight to RF | Turn body slightly to L
4 1: Close LF to RF in LSP | Continue to turn body to normal position
L hand to L side. Gradually lower to waist level
2: RF back | No turn
Continue to circle L hand clockwise over step 4 and 5. Incline body slightly L
3: Transfer weight to LF | --
return body to normal position.
4 1: Close RF to LF in Closed Position | --
Take required hold at end of this step.
Follower
2 3 4 1: Three walks fwd circling R behind Man's back RLR. End in LSP | 1/2 to R over step 1-3
2 3 4 1: Three walks fwd still circling R, LRL. End in Closed Position | 1/2 to R over step 4-6
Alternative finishing positions:
Man and Lady end to side
Man and lady end diagonally fwd in Open CPP
Man 1/8 R on step 6.
Lady 7/8 to R over step 1 to 6.
Lady end fwd toward Man's R side
Man turns body slightly R on step 6 to lead Lady towards his R side.
Lady LF fwd in line with RF.
Man and Lady fwd towards partner's R side
Man RF fwd in line with LF towards Lady's R side.
Lady LF fwd in line with RF
R Shadow Position
Man RF fwd in line with LF towards Lady's R side, and continues to turn her R.
Lady ends LF back having made 1/2 turn R on LF during preceding beat.
Adopt R shadow Hold.

Dance Central - Reverse Top
Rumba Reverse Top
This is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Start in Contact Position.
Leader
2: RF to side and slightly fwd | Up to 2 complete turns over steps 1-9
3: Swivel on ball of LF, ending LF in front of RF (Cuban Cross)
Man leave L toe in place, Lady rotates around it.
4 1: RF to side and slightly fwd
2 3 4 1 2 3 4 1: Repeat steps 2 and 3 three times
Follower
2: LF behind RF (Cuban Cross) | Up to 2 complete turns over steps 1-9
Make sure frame is good, solid, with energy. shoulder down. parallel to Man.
3: RF back and slightly to side
4 1: LF behind RF (Cuban Cross)
2 3 4 1 2 3 4 1: Repeat step 2 and 3 three times.
Note:
Because of the continuous turn, the knees will not straighten fully on steps 1 through 8. Only a slight, natural hip movement is used.

Dance Central - Spiral
Rumba Spiral Turn
Spiral Turn is a Silver Level figures. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
It is danced by the Lady and is a turn made on RF turning L or on LF turning R, allowing the other foot to cross in front without weight.
Normally a spiral turn will end with ankles crossed although the cross in the Curl is looser.
Leader
Commence in Closed Position
2: LF to side in RSP | slight body turn to R
Start like Closed Hip Twist.
Increase tone and lower L arm to R at chest level. Widen R arm in hold to R side
3: Transfer weight to RF
Retain tone in arms
4 1: Close LF to RF | slight body turn to L to normal position
Return arms to normal position and raise L hand above Lady's R shoulder to circle counterclock wise.
If preferred, Man can keep his fingers lightly in contact with Lady's waist as she dances her spiral turn.
2: RF back
L arm to L side lowering L hand to waist level
3: Transfer weight to LF | 1/8 to L over step 5 and 6
Increase tone in L arm
4 1: RF to side in Fan Position
Slightly extend L arm L hand still.
Could do either Aida ending or ending in Fan Position.
Follower
2: RF back in RSP | 1/2 to R
Start like Closed Hip Twist
Turn is commenced at end of preceding step.
3: Transfer weight to LF
Need to dance L hip more forward, don't be afraid to go forward, before rotating.
4 1: RF fwd and slightly across LF in front of Man, then turn underarm, allowing LF to cross in front without weight (Spiral Turn) | 1/4 to L, then a further 7/8 spiral turn
Lady can use extended forward walk.
Just turn 1/4 to L first, RF fwd, then spiral in front of Man
Turn is made on ball of RF with foot flat (T of LF) after getting full weight onto RF.
Turn could also be made on the R toe with heel well off the floor. (High Spiral)
At end of 1, make sure to settle R hip before stepping next step (shouldn't fall into next step.)
L arm elbow do not drop down, keep it up horizontal to keep volume/space.
L arm in front, keep volume.
2: LF fwd | 1/8 to L
3: RF fwd and slightly across, then turn to end RF back | 1/8 to L, then a further 1/2 Body turns less
Aida ending: RF fwd in line with LF, then 1/2 turn to L to end RF back in LSP.
4 1: LF back in Fan Position | Body completes turn
Aida ending: LF back in LSP.
Note: 
Alternatives:
Underturned Spiral Turn:
Man: turn 1/8 L over step 5 and 6 to end in Open Position with RF fwd.
Lady 3/4 L after she has placed step 3. End in Tandem Position with Man; Lady LF fwd,; RF fwd in line with LF, then turn 1/2 to end with RF back; then LF back.
Ending in Open Position:
Man turn 3/8 to L over step 5 and 6.
Ending in Open CPP.
End in Contact Position: Closed Spiral
Man turn 5/8 to L over step 5 and 6 to Contact Position, to end RF side and slightly fwd, overturning Lady, keeping fingers lightly in contact with Lady's waist throughout as she turns within the circle of his R arm. On step 4, lower arms to normal position.
Lady: make a complete turn L over step 4 to 6 to end LF behind RF as Cuban Cross

Spot Turns (including Switch Turns, Underarm Turns)
Rumba Spot Turns and Switch Turns
These are Newcomer Level figures. This online reference gives a detailed description of dance steps; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
This is similar to Cha Cha Cha Spot Turns (including Switch Turns, Underarm Turns) (replacing chasse with one step).
Leader
2: RF fwd (after turning 1/4 to L on preceding "&") in line with LF. Then turn 1/2 to end with RF in the back on "&" | 1/4 to L, step RF, then 1/2 to L
This is Switch Turn to Left.
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
3: Transfer weight to LF
"3&", on "&": turn 1/4 to L
4 1: RF to side | 1/4 to L over 3-5
At end of "1": turn to R.
2: LF fwd (after 1/4 to R) in line with RF, then turn 1/2 to end with LF in the back | 1/4 to R, step LF, then 1/2 to R
This is Switch Turn to Right.
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
3: Transfer weight to RF
on "&", turn 1/4 to R
4 1: LF to side | 1/4 to R over 3-5
Follower
2: LF fwd (after turning 1/4 to R on preceding "&") in line with RF, Then turn 1/2 to R to end with LF in the back on "&" | 1/4 to R, step LF, Then 1/2 to R on "&"
This is Switch Turn to Left (because Man is turning L)
End of preceding step on '&', full commit the weight on RF, settle, pressure on LF, energy is forward, tail bone down; then swivel on RF, do quick impulse, turn 1/4 to R. Pay attention to foot position, LF turned out, not turned in (sometimes due to over turning to R on this step.)
Different from New Yorker: Fully commit weight on front foot before doing the half turn to R using Forward Walk Turn action.. (Difference in lead compared to New Yorker.)
Arm option: left hand on waist, or left arm wrap around the back. R arm can be held in front of tummy, palm facing tummy. Hands should not have other extra unintended actions, go directly to waist, pure and simple (if that's the choice).
On "&" after the turn, compress on left side. (Finishing with hips to the side, then, quick impulse, it will twist to move spine to RF in next step.)
To achieve the effect of "quick impulse" - sudden action:
body turns more continuously at same speed,
head: do head spotting and fast changing head direction
leg/knee delays the turn a bit and then turn much faster and body and leg arrives at the final position at the same time.
3: Transfer weight on to RF
Options:
before "3", there is a moment the R leg is bent, then straighten before stepping on floor.
or: Keep R leg straight the whole time.
Hold this a bit longer and have weight fully transfer to RF (see Latin Technique Swivel Action) and make sure to finish the diagonal pendulum swing before doing twist on '&'.
On "&", quick impulse, twist, turn 1/4 to R (turn before stepping next step)
When turning to right, it's Right Side leading.
Styling: when moving forward, arms would go back (think of wind blowing at the arms.)
4 1: LF to side | 1/4 to R over 3-5
Turn/swivel at end of previous beat. Track LF underneath and then place under the shoulder to the side, not too big step. It's a smooth step, do not pause in middle. "4" is a side step (after the turning to face partner), do not make it a forward step, then turn. 
At end of this step, work RF down to ground, develop the foot more.
For underarm turn, at the "&": twist L hip, pulling R hip through, R arm will close in front of face (to left side). Keep L shoulder fwd side leading.
2: RF fwd (after 1/4 to L) in line with LF, Then turn 1/2 to L ending with RF in the back | 1/4 to L, then 1/2 to L
Switch Turn to Right (Man is turning to R)
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
When turning at the end of "2", body is not square, use Left Side Leading.
If Lady's R arm is connected to Man, Lady will feel R arm closing window.
Static body design: hands statically on hips while turning and extend arms to side on 41.
More flexible and fluid design: When turning, L arm extend to the side and as body rotate, arm will warp in front of tummy, R hand on hip or R arm wrap around the back.
For Switch Turns, if Man is still hold Lady's R hand, Lady: Put LH on hip first, (end of previous beat) then raising L arm to side during "2", at beginning of count "3", L arm goes in front of chest. (and Man will release hold to Lady's RH, Lady's R arm wrap around back.)
3: Transfer weight to LF 
on "&", turn 1/4 to L
4 1: RF to side | 1/4 to L over 3-5
When followed by underarm turn or Alemana, L arm will be straight out to the side.
Note:
Spot Turns: 3 forward steps danced solo by Man and/or Lady, circling to L or R.
Spot Turns can be danced as Switch Turns. Start in Closed Position. No hold, or L to R or R to L hand hold.
Turning action: could be a Forward Walk Turn action, or a brushing action where the free foot closes to standing foot, brush and then do a fwd walk.
Different from underarm turn, for spot turns,
count "2": lady turns about 1/8", diagonally "&" RF brush LF
count "3": lady turns about 1/4, RF fwd
if preferred, the ball of the foot may remain in place while turn is made around this foot.
count "4 1": finish turn to face partner
last step could be fwd or to side depending on distance between couple.
Often, one partner is doing Spot Turn, while the other does Alternative Basic or Cuban Rock, or 3 steps of a Closed Basic.
Spot Turn is a common ending for New York, Hand to Hand, Fencing and may other figures.
Amount of Turns: complete turn, or 7/8 or 3/4, depending on starting and ending position: open PP, Open CPP, facing partner.
Spot Turn or Switch to L (Lady to R) could end in Open Position, taking L to R hand hold at step 3, taking step 3 fwd leading Lady to step back.

Rumba Shoulder To Shoulder
This is a Newcomer Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of dance steps; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
This is similar to Cha Cha Cha Shoulder to Shoulder.
Normally commenced in Closed Position.
Leader
2: LF fwd to Lady's L side | No Turn
Body has no turns, frame is facing partner. Only lower body turns to step Outside Partner.
This is Left Side Shoulder to Shoulder.
LF in CBMP. R shoulder is quite fwd.
Finishing hip just like Close Basic.
3: Transfer Weight to RF
ball flat.
4 1: LF to side, ending LF side and slightly fwd | 1/4 to L over 3-5
(feet turn only)
2: RF fwd towards Lady's R side | No turn
This is Right Side Shoulder to Shoulder.
3: Transfer weight to LF
4 1: RF to side, ending RF side and slightly fwd | 1/4 to R over 8-10
Follower
2: RF back | No turn
Body has no turns, frame is facing partner. Only lower body turns to step Outside Partner.
Keep shoulder parallel to Man.
This is Left Side Shoulder to Shoulder.
3: Transfer weight to LF towards Man's side
Twist R side to push LF forward. Then, pendulum action.
4 1: RF to side, ending RF side and slightly back | 1/4 to L over 3-5
Twist, pendulum.
2: LF back | No turn
This is Right Side Shoulder to Shoulder.
Foot position is in CBMP. Also called Latin Cross. 
CBM: take shoulder of free leg to standing leg.
When foot is stepped cross the line, it's strong CBMP.
3: Transfer weight to RF towards Man's R side
Twist LF side, and pendulum.
4 1: LF to side, ending LF side and slightly back | 1/4 to R over 8-10
Note:
Shoulder To Shoulder Left side:
Following Hockey Stick (Man move slightly towards Lady's L side on last step), Shoulder to Shoulder may be danced with L to R or double hand hold (end in Open PP, then followed by Spot or Switch turn to L)
Man keeps the L hand higher than usual to prevent Lady moving too far back on last step of the Hockey Stick, although the couple will be further apart than when dancing the figure in Closed Position.
When danced this way, it can end in Open PP, Lady no turn.
Similarly for Shoulder To Shoulder Right side.

Dance Central - Three Alemanas
Rumba Three Alemanas
Rumba Three Alemanas is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the different variation of the dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
This can start in Fan Position. It could also start from Open Position.
2 3 4 1 2: Same as Alemana | No turn
Start in Fan Position.
3: LF fwd and slightly leftwards | No turn
4 1: Close RF to LF in RSP | Slight body turn to R
Increase tone and lower L arm to R. End with L arm braced.
2: LF to side with part weight | Slight body turn to L to normal position
Raise L hand above lady's R shoulder and circle it counter-clockwise.
3: Transfer weight to RF | No turn
Lower L hand to shoulder level
4 1: Close LF to RF | --
Brace L arm in normal position
2 3 4 1: Same as steps 4-6 of Alemana
Raise L hand above Lady's R shoulder, then continue steps 4-6 of Alemana.
Follower
2341 2341: Same as Alemana.
Start in Fan Position. 1 1/4 turn to R over steps 3 to 6, then a further 1/2 on step 6. 
May start in Open Position, in which case, 2341 goes straight fwd to Man (41 does not step to side of Man, it is straight to Man).
Remember turning technique: body first, then hip, center in, close ribs, thighs tight
First Alemana, ending 2341, step 6: 
LF fwd in line with RF towards Man's R side, fwd step can be shorter. then turn to end LF back in RSP.
weight is onto whole LF faster on 4, giving it more stability and more time to twist to R. R hip high, open up to R. 
Then, more twisting to R, point RF, R leg very straight, the twisting hip to R makes left knee bend a little, but don't think too much about sitting butt out. The feeling is prepare to go into RF forward step, not having to get up from sitting position. 
R leg very straight, make the line while twisting to R. LF should swivel and turn enough. Upper body is winding up to get ready to turn the other direction (L)
create space in belly area, belly button to spine, release the R hip down, thighs tight together, upper body fwd. Keep head aligned with spine, don't twist or shape head. Create the K shape.
Twisting feels slower.
Lady L arm up while twisting (can also collect L arm in front of body, forearm vertical). R arm at center waist level, RH connected with Man, R elbow out (not down), While settling/rotating R hip to R, it creates pull tension with Man's LH, but do not pull R arm artificially. 
2: RF slightly leftwards, then turn underarm allowing LF to cross loosely in front of RF without weight (Spiral Turn) | 1/8 to L, then a further 3/4 to L
Small step, onto RF first (don't do shoulders yet), then turn shoulders.
Different styles of spiral turn: wrap foot (wing the foot, do not sickle foot), high spiral, locking ankles.
2 onto RF, options: do spiral: hold head, turning body to left, do a spiral turn, or direct fwd walk turn, pivot, then swivel.
Shoulder is to right, not turned completely flat.
Some do this more more diagonal, some more straight in front of Man. 
3: LF fwd | 1/8 to L
Almost feel like stepping away from Man. LF fwd underneath shoulder, keep very small steps, don't travel too much, do not step big outside body. 
Settle hip, Then, do flexible turn.
4 1: RF fwd | 1/2 to L
The turn is made at end of previous beat.
Pressed Fwd Walk. Lady should bring her weight well fwd, responding to Man's tone in his L arm. pressure in RH (to Man's LH) . 
Open L hip up, stretching the body (L hip back, R chest fwd to Man), release/drop R hip down.
L arm shoot out to L side.
2 3 4 1: Same as steps 4-6 of Alemana
A complete turn to R over steps 10-12.
Note:
There are a lot of turns/twists. Step onto foot, axis moved to the new standing foot, turn/twist around axis (shoulder first, hip follows), release free hip, move to new axis and start over.
One coach's imagery: rotisserie chicken on a skewer. The axis/skewer does not change, shift, break or move. chicken rotates around skewer.

Leader

3: Transfer weight to RF | --
Contract L arm over step 2 and 3 to end with L hand close to L hip.
4 1: Closed LF to RF, slightly back. End in TP | --
Brace L arm towards end of step 3, then release hold. Place hands on Lady's shoulders at end of her turn.
2: RF back | --
Retain tone in both arms
3: Transfer weight to LF | --
Retain tone
4 1: Close RF to LF in TP | --
Increase tone, R arm slightly fwd, L arm slightly back, then release hold. Place hands on Lady's shoulder at end of her turn.
2: LF diag. fwd with part weight | Slight body turn to R
Retain tone in both arms. Press Line can be danced. (See Advanced Hip Twist)
3: Transfer weight to RF | --
Retain tone. Footwork: flat.
4 1: Close LF to RF | Slight body turn to L to normal position
Increase tone in L arm, slightly fwd, R arm slightly back, then release hold.
2: RF back | No turn
No hold.
3: Transfer weight to LF | --
4 1: Close RF to LF. End in Closed Position. | --
Take required hold.
Follower
2: RF back | No Turn.
Start in Open Position. 
More rotation, take center really really back, squeeze R side, counter balance with Man. Hand: pull tension with Man, delayed coming toward Man.
3: Transfer weight to LF | --
4 1: RF fwd, then turn, closing LF to RF without weight in TP | 1/2 turn to R
RF fwd, lady dancing R side more forward to Man, step onto RF, bring weight well fwd onto RF. 
Start turning shoulder to R, body twisted. At very end of count 1, turn hips, turn on ball of RF, foot flat, pressure on ball of LF.
Do not go up, which will lose energy. Try to keep flat, grounded.
2: Transfer weight to LF | No turn
23: in place hip action, figure 8
Style option: both knees bent and rotate to left, L hip high.
3: Transfer weight to RF | --
rotate to R, R hip high, straightening R leg
4 1: Replace weight to LF, then turn, closing RF to LF slightly back without weight in TP | Full turn to L
4: weight onto LF, L leg straightened, shoulder wind up to R first, before prepping to turn by turning upper body first to L.
1: turn body first, then turn hips, all turns around spine, ending L leg straight, collect R leg, R knee drape over, using upper thigh muscle to stop the turn; Remember to: collect center in, thighs tight, "close the ribs", weight on balls of foot
LF flat, pressure on ball of RF. 
2: RF diag back with part weight | No turn
Often danced as side or side slightly back cucarachas
Breathe and do not tense up body, making it look rigid when doing steps faster. 
Eyes - focus on a spot in each turning direction, with focus, body is more compact
3: Transfer weight to LF | --
Foot flat. Squeeze left side, on & collect/brush RF to LF before striking out RF on 4 next.
4 1: RF fwd, then turn with LF trailing behind without weight | 1/2 to R
4: RF forward, dancing R side forward, chest is more diagonal; fully send weight to R hip, continue turning shoulder to R, then finish half turn to R. For this and next few turns, use flexible turn (brush RF to LF) to show off leg movement
1: end of beat, turn on ball of RF, foot flat. Inside edge of toe of LF. hip needs to turn more, finish R hip. R shoulder fwd, it helps to stop the turn. After initiating turn with turning R shoulder, R shoulder more or less stays in place, so R shoulder ends fwd and create counter with settling R hip. Do not over turn R shoulder to R. 
2 3 4 1: 3 fwd walks LRL. End in Closed Position | A complete turn to R over these 4 counts.
2: LF fwd Forward Walk Turn, 1/2 turn to R
3: RF swivel, 1/2 to R
41: LF fwd to Man's R side
Note:
Lady's alternative steps for steps 10 to 12:
2, 3: Dance 1-2 of Switch Turn to R (1/2 turn). Then, count 41: LF fwd, after having turned another 1/2 to R.
No turn on step 9, then turn 1/4 to R into step 1 and 2 of LF Side Cucaracha on count 2 3; then LF fwd. Then, LF fwd after having turned a further 1/4 to R.
Finishing Position: Man and lady can step fwd towards partners R side, followed by different Hip Twists.
Fan Ending:
Man:
Step 9 (41): Close LF to RF, 1/8 to L; Increase tone, L arm slighlty fwd. R arm slightly back; then release hold allowing R hand to slide down Lady's R arm to R to R hand hold.
2: RF back, no turn; R arm towards L side, then change to L to R hand hold.
3: Transfer weight to LF, 1/8 to L, over count 3 4 1; increase tone in L arm.
41: RF to side in Fan Position. Slightly extend L arm.
Lady:
Step 10 -12: dance the finishing steps for Closed Hip Twist to end in Fan Position.
2: LF fwd passing in front of Man's body
3: RF fwd in line with LF, then turn 1/2 to L, body turns less; ending RF back
41: LF back in Fan position, body completes turn.

2: Close RF to LF | No turn

3: LF fwd | --
4 1: RF fwd | --
2: LF fwd | 1/8 to L

Hands could be crossed in front of body. Or, Man could change to R to R hand hold. See notes below.
4 1: LF back in RSP | --
R arm to side.
2: RF back | --
This is often the starting point of sliding door. 
RF heel down, but make sure L upper body is still stretched forward, do hip action to roll R hip back, but do not move weight too far back, keep upper body forward. R upper body rotate to R.
Common preceding entry: When previous figure finishes with Lady's LF fwd on 41 e.g. Alemana - in which case, sharp 1/2 turn to R (turn body first, keep legs tight, squeeze; take center in) then, RF back. create elasticity. 
R arm go up, next to ear (but keep space), flip wrist. Fingers same plane as palm, don't break fingers (except for middle finger styling) Keep R shoulder blade down. Body rotated to R.
There are different hand hold options:
Lady's LH to Man's LH (L elbow flat with arm, not down), Lady's RH go straight up on side.
double hand hold: Lady's LH to Man's LH, Lady's RH to Man's RH
Lady's LH on her own left ribs, L elbow forward (don't hit Man), 
3: Transfer weight to LF | --
Hip Twist action: Rotate R hip more back high, counter balance with rib cage forward. Then twist (Use swivel technique, contract L ribs, rotate L) and upper body will rotate the other way (to R) to counter the hip to L. Shoulders stay about same to front, but rib cage is twisting against hip direction.
Wheel on R elbow, R hand and upper arm behind head, do it completely, not half way; then elbow in front, then down to side of body 
4 1: RF fwd and across in TP | --
Pressed walk on 41. 
R leg/RF close to the standing LF first (R knee drape over) before it opens to pressed walk position. (Anna Kovalova's tip for Lady.)
RF leg is diagonally leftward. Do not step RF too big forward, make it more cross LF, more rotation to L.
RF needs to be turned out to R (turn knee out, R heel in to left & up) 
R leg is pressing fwd, L leg is back straight, stretching/creating space between the 2 legs. L hip rotated back L.
R side of body is forward, create diagonal stretch between L hip rotating back and R side of body stretching fwd.
Arms: Focus on working the body, not "working the hands". arm and hands movement comes from body movement. Arm movement to right syncs with when hip rotates to L.
R arm full arm to side, or 
R upper arm vertical next to body, flip lower arm to side like carrying a purse. Or,
R hand on hip. 
End of 1: Straight R leg, RF slide diagonally fwd, bigger R hip rotation which brings LF in, collect LF before stepping LF out (but seamless, no stopping LF).
2: LF to side | --
Before stepping LF to side, first hip twist (swivel technique) on RF, collect LF next to RF, collect thighs together, L knee drape over R leg, create nice line and then step LF out. (seamless, passing, no stop)
Option: Traditionally LF goes to side, current day, LF is taken more diagonally fwd to give it 3D (nice picture from every angle) and it makes next turn to R easier as well). 
L arm extend to left, squeeze side, full straight L arm action from side to front, leading with wrist, then hand follows. Then bent and collect L arm (don't bend too early). (R arm elbow in.) Cucaracha action
Finish L hip high. 
3: Transfer weight to RF in TP | --
L elbow in, R elbow down.
At end of 3, collect LF to RF first before stepping back on 4 
4 1: LF back and across in RSP | --
If continuing, man normal hold Lady's L wrist at this step.
Advanced version: at end of 3, 1/2 turn to R , LF fwd on "4", End of "1" half turn to R, RF back, continue with Advanced hip twist or or another Sliding Door.
Note:
Alternative hold:
Man: 
keep L hand below chest level, taking R to R hand hold at end of 3, releasing hold with L hand.
Then, lead Lady with R hand to pass in front of him to R side, releasing hold at end of 5, allowing R hand to trail along Lady's R arm until it reaches her R shoulder blade o step 6.
Then, take Lady's L hand into his L hand. (R shadow position, hold 1) Retain this hold for steps 7 to 12.
When following with Lady's Spiral, man lead Lady into her spiral turn by lowering L hand, then immediately gaining normal Fan or Open Position hold at end of last step.
When following with Progressive Walks Forward, retain R Shadow hold.
Variation w/ Lady's R turn to hip twist position:
2: Cucaracha LF to side, whole foot
3: weight to RF, LF push floor, L heel lift, L hip up, hip diagonally to R and then rotate to R, collect LF near R ankle and then step forward to Man's R side on 4. 
4: LF forward to Man's R side. L arm to side, R hand on Man's chest.

Dance Central - Jive Technique
Timing
6 count timing: 1a 2 3a 4 5 6 (Beat value: 3/4 1/4 1 3/4 1/4 1 1 1)
Common mistake is to dance 1/2 1/2 1 1/2 1/2 1 1 1)
Accent on the "2"s (i.e. 2, 4, 6 - contract and kick)
8 count timing: 1a 2 3 4 5a 6 7 8 (Beat value: 3/4 1/4 1 1 3/4 1/4 1 1 1)
Posture
Both feet always inside edge, it hurts more than the other Latin dances.
Weight is on ball of feet. Lean body pitched slightly forward. Butt sticking out a bit, but not exaggerated. 
The head stays in one place, the hips and the feet swing from side to side.
Basic Actions
Jive actions are more compact. No Rumba style figure 8, there is no time. Just pendulum action from side to side, not diagonal forward like Rumba. 
Knees are inward. 
Chasse: Ball - Ball Change action. Example start with RF chasse to right:
Timing: 3/4 1/4 1 (3a 4)
Feet IE of foot first, not Whole Foot. 
Thighs tight toward center.
1st side step: very small step, almost next to the other foot, only as much as you can swing the hips under shoulder.
Body leading to side first, while hip is still left "behind", body and hip counter balance each other, opposition.
3&: on &: has small, tiny, impulse of tummy contraction action (without involving shoulder), the contraction pull the trailing foot in on 'a' next. The scooping action with center tummy makes the other foot move/close. 
a: move the trailing foot next to standing foot.
4: finishing chasse, not on straight leg, but on soft knee, slower, smoother, change of hip swing and direction. 
&: free knee up (L knee), articulate LF down, while body slightly fwd, R heel up
Rock step (Link):
very small, no time for big step. straighten the leg, but do not lock the knee.
do not move body weight all the way back, leave weight forward, front knee bent. back foot just kisses the floor.
"Hop" is on accent (end of 2 4 6)
Contract core (abs). The standing foot will slide slightly back as a result.
Imagine getting mud off the shoe. Weight forward. Knee up, point foot, hip tilt back, kick your own bottom, leg bounce off.
"Swing" action: This is not a simple side to side action. It contains both pendulum and metronome action.
1: downward, pendulum
a: metronome to make a a little circle and come down again
2: pendulum up again
Kick-Ball-Change
This is often used in place of basic rock action.
Example: kick ball change on R side:
Previous beat 'a': contract, 'hop': LF slide slightly back, lift R leg, R knee up, RF kick bottom, tilt hip, upper body forward, height of the head should not change (head should not pop up much).
1: kick leg straight (downward action)
a: Ball: Partial weight on ball of RF, contract and LF will "hitch", kick bottom, getting mud off shoe before stepping forward in next step.
2: Change step: place on LF underneath the spine, directly drop down with weight, much quicker (than popping and then down)
Warmup Exercise
Start with weight on RF, LF free.
Chasse to Left (1a 2), Chasse to Right (3a 4). Repeat 1 time (5a6 7a 8)
Chasse forward (1a 2, 3a 4, 5a 6, 7a 8)
LF Forward kick (1), side kick(2), Sailor Shuffle (LF cross behind RF, RF closes to LF, LF step to side. Timing: 3a 4)
RF Forward kick (1) , side kick (2), Sailor Shuffle (RF cross behind LF, LF closes to RF, RF step to side. Timing: 3a 4)
Repeat forward/side kick/sailor shuffle 1 time. (1 2 3a 4, 5 6 7a 8)
Chasse backward (1a 2, 3a 4, 5a 6, 7a 8)
LF Backward kick (1), side kick(2), Sailor Shuffle (LF cross behind RF, RF closes to LF, LF step to side. Timing: 3a 4)
RF Backward kick (1) , side kick (2), Sailor Shuffle (RF cross behind LF, LF closes to RF, RF step to side. Timing: 3a 4)
Repeat backward/side kick/sailor shuffle 1 time. (1 2 3a 4, 5 6 7a 8)
[Repeat whole combination again.]
Variation: Forward/side/cross back slow, then fast; back/side/cross forward slow, then fast
LF forward kick (1), side kick (2), LF cross behind RF, hold (3 4) with weight
RF forward kick (5), side kick (6), RF cross behind LF, hold ( 7 8) with weight
LF forward kick (1), side kick (2), LF cross behind RF (a), RF forward kick (3), RF side kick (4), RF cross behind LF (a)
Repeat: LF forward kick (1), side kick (2), LF cross behind RF (a), RF forward kick (3), RF side kick (4), RF cross behind LF (a)
LF back kick (1), side kick (2), LF cross in front of RF (3 4) with weight
RF back kick (1), side kick (2), RF cross in front of LF (3 4) with weight
LF back kick (1), side kick (2), LF cross in front of RF (a), RF kick back (3), RF side kick (4), RF cross in front of LF (a)
Repeat: LF back kick (1), side kick (2), LF cross in front of RF (a), RF kick back (3), RF side kick (4), RF cross in front of LF (a)

Dance Central - American Spin
Jive American Spin
American Spin is a Bronze Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, movement, a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Start in Open Position. R to R hand hold.
Leader
QQ: 1-2 of Link LR | no turn
QaQ: close LF near RF, slightly fwd for compact chasse LRL
retract R arm to lead lady fwd.
increase tone in arm on 5, then turn lady to R, release hold
QaQ: compact chasse RLR. End in open position
regain L to R or R to R hand hold at end of lady's turn.
Follower
QQ: 1-2 of Link RL | no turn
QaQ: fwd chasse RLR, then turn R on RF
no turn on 3-5, then 1/2 to R on RF
Lady may turn up to 3/4 R on RF on 5, completing turn over 6-7.
Lady should respond tone in Man's arm by increasing her tone in her R arm, taking her weight well forward to lean against Man's hand.
QaQ: LF diag back to chasse LRL, to end LF back in open position | 1/4 to R over 6-8.
Notes:
Variations:
Start in L to R hand hold.
Retain hold and lead lady to spin under raise arm on 5. Lower at end of lady's turn.

Dance Central - Catapult
Jive Catapult
Jive Catapult is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, movement, a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Start in Open Position, Right to Right hand hold.
Leader
QQ: 1,2 of Link LR | No turn
QaQ: Compact chasse LRL | No turn
R hand to R side. On step 5, raise R hand and start to circle it counterclockwise over Lady's head.
QaQ: Compact chasse RLR. End in Tandem Position, Lady behind. | No turn
Continue to circle R hand counter-clockwise, then lower it behind back, taking Lady's L hand in L hand behind back.
Q: LF fwd in Tandem Position | No turn
Slightly extend arms behind back.
Q: Replace weight to RF, having moved it slightly rightwards | No turn
Slightly retract arms
QaQ: Compact chasse LRL | No turn
Release hold with R hand and take L hand fwd in front of body to lead Lady well fwd. Lead Lady to turn R and release hold with L hand.
QaQ: Compact chasse RLR. End in Open Position | No turn
Return to L to R or R to R hand hold at end of Lady's turn.
Man may turn 1/4 to L over steps 14-16, then Lady would turn 1/4 to R.
Follower
QQ: 1,2 of Link RL | No turn.
QaQ: Fwd chasse RLR to Man's R side | 1/8 to L over steps 3-5
QaQ: Turning chasse underarm LRL. End LF back in Tandem Position behind Man. | 3/8 to L over steps 6 and 7
Turn is started at end of previous beat.
Q: RF back in Tandem Position. | No turn
Lady should feel she is pulling away from Man. This is the Catapult part of the figure.
Q: Replace weight to LF | No turn
QaQ: Fwd chasse RLR passing Man's L side, then turn to R on RF | 1/8 to R between step 12 and 13, then 7/8 to R on RF
QaQ: LF to side and slightly back to chasse LRL. End LF back in Open Position. | 1/2 to R over steps 14-16.

Dance Central - Chicken Walks
Jive Chicken Walks
The Chicken Walks is a Gold level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, movement, a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Start in Open Position.
Leader
Q: LF back, small step, with partial weight
Increase tone in L arm to prevent Lady stepping back.
Merengue action is used on steps 1-3.
Step 1: R knee is firmly straightened on 2nd half of preceding step, making R hip to move back and to R. Then LF is palced back on ball of foot with knee flexed.
Q: Taking full weight to LF, RF back, small step, partial weight
End of previous step: Lower LF heel, straighten L knee, L hip moves back to L. RF is placed back on ball with knee flexed.
Q: Take full weight to RF, LF back, small step, with partial weight
repeat. Man's straightening and flexing of knees indicates to Lady to turn.
Q: Take full weight to LF, RF back, small step
Follower
Q: RF point fwd in line with LF, no weight, R leg straight. L knee flexed. | Hips turn to R
RF on OE of toe pointing fwd. Keep weight/upper body forward. Keep LH at waist. Left shoulder forward.
a: RF step toward LF | Hips to normal
R shoulder forward.
Q: Point LF fwd, R knee flexed | Hips to L
LF on OE of toe pointing fwd
a: F steps toward RF | Hips normal
Q: RF point fwd, L knee flexed | Hips to R
RF on OE of toe pointing fwd
a: RF closes to LF | Hips normal
Q: LF fwd | No turn
Step onto LF and rise (going to RLR chasse.)
Notes:
Timing: Other combinations of "S"s and "Q"s can be used. Examples: "Sa Sa Sa S", "Qa Qa Qa Q", "Sa Sa Qa Qa Qa Q"
When dancing slow, L hand and arm can add styling.
Slow or Quick swivel action can be danced.
