Dance Central - Natural Fleckerl
Viennese Waltz Natural Fleckerl
The Viennese Waltz Natural Fleckerl is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Man
Commencing facing LOD (any other alignment is possible).
The figure can complete one or two full turns, depending upon the dancers.
1: RF fwd btwn Lady's feet | Facing DC Against LOD | HB | 1/8 to R btwn preceeding step and 1 | Nil | CBM | Nil
2: LF to side and slightly fwd with 1/2 weight, continue to turn on RF | Facing DW | B | 1/2 to R btwn 1 and 2 | - | -
3: Continue to turn on RF and take full weight on RF. LF held behind RF | Facing Against LOD | BH | 3/8 to R btwn 2 and 3 | - | -
1: LF to side and slightly fwd | Facing DC Against LOD | HB| 1/8 to R btwn 3 and 4 | - | - 
2: RF crosses behind LF, 1/2 weight (a type of twist turn) | Facing DW | B | 1/2 to R btwn 4 and 5 | - | -
3: Untwist on RF then LF to side, small step | Facing Against LOD | BH | 3/8 to R btwn 5 and 6 | - | -
Lady
1: LF to side and slightly fwd | Facing DW | HB | 1/8 to R btwn preceding step and 1 | - | -
Continue turning movement on RF from previous step, do not put LF down too early (that will cut down the turn).
Always try to maintain parallel to partner. Don't think about turning shoulders. Don't let body fall back. Keep tail bone underneath body. This is right turning figure, keep right side positive.
This is heel lead. This is because Lady is on Man's R side, has good clearance and can provide powerful strong action. (On Reverse Fleckerl, Lady is underturned and is not in leading position and so the footwork is BHB)
There is no super big shape because of the amount of rotation and speed, but keep center to Man. Think about L elbow/arm going forward left.
2: RF crosses behind LF, 1/2 weight (a type of twist turn) | Facing DC Against LOD | B | 1/2 to R btwn 1 and 2 | - | -
Pull ab muscle, keep body lined up and forward. Do not stick butt out. No big shape. 
Use twisting action through the ball of RF to allow the body momentum to carry on.
Keep momentum going. Keep L side fwd, but do not lift L elbow. Arm flat.
3: Untwist on RF, then LF to side, small step | Facing Against LOD | BH | 3/8 to R btwn 2 and 3 | - | -
1: RF fwd, slightly cross, btwn Man's feet | Facing DW | BH | 1/8 to R btwn 3 and 4 | - | CBM | -
2: LF to side and slightly fwd with 1/2 weight | Facing DC Against LOD | B | 1/2 to R btwn 4 and 5 | - | -
3: RF crosses loosely in front of LF | Facing Against LOD | BH | 3/8 to R btwn 5 and 6 | - | -
It's not tight cross at ankles, it's loosely crossing at knee level.
Note:
The figure can commence anywhere where previous figure has ended. 
It can rotate 1 up to 2 revolutions depending on the couple. 
For both Man and Lady, there is a strong rotation, so there must be a foot swivel on the ball of the flat foot on each step.
Middle of body forward, side to Man. Shaping is not as big as other dances, focus on rotation.

Man/Lady (LF Forward Change Step)
Start with Man facing DW, turning 1/8 to step LF forward along LOD.
1: LF fwd | FLOD | HT | 1/8 to L btwn preceding step and 1 | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1
2: RF diagonally fwd | FDC | T | 1/8 to L btwn 1 and 2 | Sway(L) | up on 2
3: LF closes to RF | FDC | TH | Sway(L) | up on 3, lower e/o 3
Lady/Man (RF Backward Change Step)
Start with Lady BDW, turning 1/8 to L, RF stepping BLOD.
1: RF back | BLOD | TH | 1/8 to L btwn preceding step and 1 | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1, NFR

Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing LOD (or DC) | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1
If commenced DC, turn 1/4 between 1-2.
Q: RF to side | Backing DW | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | up
&: LF back in CBMP | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | up

Follower
S: RF back | BLOD (or BDC) | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1, NFR
Curve the step. Connect R shoulder down to R hip (turning the dial). 
Preparing for L turn (Lady is inside of turn): Lower part of body is rotary, upper body is on sway plane. For left turning figures, upper body turns less. L shoulder positive.
Keep left side strongly to the Man all through the steps.
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing to LOD | T | 1/2 to L between 1-2, Body turns less | Sways(R) | up
The movement is still same direction back, foot is pointing LOD.
Swing L hip and L side through an upward diagonal (push from the R hip), do not put head weight forward too quickly. Feel the lightness. Body stretched.
&: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing LOD | T | slight body turn | Sway(R) | up
Q: LF fwd | Facing LOD | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Changing from L side positive to R side positive.
Feather ending.
Q: RF side | BDW | TH | 3/8 turn to L | 
Lengthen the R side. Take head to L. Turn the feet. Giving/keeping upper body and arms to partner. 
Q: LF back CBMP | BDW | TH

&: RF back, R side leading | Backing DC | T | Slight turn to R between 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up

Dance Central - Reverse Turn
Reverse Turn - V Waltz
Viennese Waltz Reverse Turn
The Viennese Waltz Reverse Turn is a Bronze figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Note that Lady's steps are really the same as Man's but doing 2nd half first, then 1st half of Man's steps.
Man
Start with Man facing DW, turning 1/8 to step LF forward along LOD.
1: LF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | 1/8 to L between preceding step and 1 | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise at end of 1
Commencing alignment is DW, start with 1/8 turn L, then step. LF straight, don't turn out LF.
2: RF to side and slightly back | Backing Wall | T | 1/4 L btwn 1 and 2 | Sway(L) | Cont to rise on 2 and 3
Instead of thinking side step, it's more of a forward step, then turn.
RF should track under LF and then forward, Avoid rondes.
3: LF crosses in front of RF | BDW | TH | 1/8 L btwn 2 and 3 | Sway(L) | up on 3, lower at end of 3
At end of step 2, RF is turning on ball of foot, causing LF to cross, don't gap LF and RF to much, two feet should be parallel.
1: RF back and slightly to side | BLOD | TH | 1/8 L btwn 3 and 4 | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 4, NFR
2: LF to side | Pointing DW | T | 3/8 L btwn 4 and 5, body turns less | Sway(R) | body rise on 5 and 6
3: RF closes to LF | FDW | Flat | body completes turn | Sway(R) | Lower e/o 6
Settle.
Note:
Man may hold the preceding sway slightly at the beginning of steps 1 and 4.
Lady
Starting BDW, then turn 1/8 to L.
1: RF back and slightly to side | BLOD | TH | 1/8 L btwn preceding step and Count 1 | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 1, NFR
Toe slightly turned in. Stretch head to L, but keeping L side body to Man.
Be careful not to turn shoulder to left as hip girdle area is turning left. Body turns less than hips. Left side stretched very long, upward angle, presenting into Man's right side. Left side is positive. 
2: LF to side | Pointing DW | T | 3/8 L btwn 1 and 2, body turns less | Sway(R) | body rise on 2 and 3
Track with leg, do not leave out and block Man.
Do not pop up. Keep L knee flexed when drawing RF in, which will help staying flat. Focus on bigger movement.
Do not take head too quickly to LF. Keep upward, leftward poise. Stretch to left. Create space with Man, Keep the volume.
3: RF closes to LF | FDW | Flat | body completes turn | Sway(R) | Lower e/o 3
Settle. Lower to neutral position.
1: LF fwd | LOD | HT | 1/8 L btwn 3 and 4 | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 4
Straight forward down LOD. It appears to step right at Man, the fact that he's turning as well, it will work out. Don't try to step to side, it will create gap.
Big step, drive, keep flat, but move more.
LF could have slightly turn out, but not too much.
No shape or shape slightly (not too much) toward inside of the turn, lengthen the opposite side, don't collapse left side, help to make next step bigger.
Take head and spine to LF. Left side is positive.
It's easy to loose R arm, R hand pressure to Man. Make sure arm is in front of body, extend arm and keep RH pressure.
2: RF to side | Backing Wall | T | 1/4 L btwn 4 and 5 | Sway(L) | cont to rise on 5 and 6
Swivel on LF and push off LF to make the side step bigger.
RF track under LF (no rondes), then forward, then turn on ball (which makes it a equivalent of a side step).
RF should be quite fwd down LOD first, (then turned to be BW), do not veer off LOD to right side otherwise, after crossing LF in front RF, the feet are going to be too far apart.
RF continue to turn when LF draw crosses in front (in next step).
3: LF crosses in front of RF | BDW | TH | 1/8 L btwn 5 and 6 | Sway(L) | up on 6, lower e/o 6
RF continues to swivel on "3", then LF crosses in front.
The two feet should be parallel, BDW. If RF is turned out, it means RF didn't continue to swivel.
Settle with weight on LF (front foot). L side positive, do not square off shoulder.
Note:
The movement is straight down line of dance. (Linear rather than circular.)
To avoid dizziness: (instead of spotting on one spot), spot different spots on the wall.
Feet should keep solid contact with floor. it helps to provide control/balance and makes steps smoother.
Stretch more to L, keep the head volume, give arms to Man, both arms forward which counters head shaping more left. (Tendency is to shift to Man's space).
Stay more flat. Keep flexed knees.
Don't do double lowering. Settle at end of 3 (this is the "neutral" position with flexed knees.). Next step, because of spreading the legs, it will naturally go lower. Do not do another "lower" and then step.
Reverse Turn either has light shaping to inside of the turn, or some top dancers do not do shaping at all. 
Shaping lengthens opposite side for bigger stride, shape forward, do not collapse the other side.
Shaping allows for longer step on step 2 and 5.
Reverse turn is normally used to travel down long side, and Natural Turn on short side. It's easier to underturn Natural Turn to go around corners than overturning Reverse Turn.
Before getting ready to use Change Step, last Reverse Turn will have smaller movement, maybe some shape. where Lady can turn head to R.
Preceding Figures
LF Backward Change (Natural to Reverse)
RF Forward Change (Natural to Reverse)
Reverse Fleckerl
Reverse Turn
Following Figures
LF Forward Change (Reverse To Natural)
Reverse Turn
Reverse Fleckerl
Contra Check
RF Backward Change (Reverse To Natural) (after 1-3 Reverse Turn, do Backward Change, then 4-6 of Natural Turn)

Dance Central - Foxtrot Technique
See Also: Standard Technique page for general technique for International standard dances.
Foxtrot Movement
Foxtrot has many similarities compared to Waltz, but it is a very different dance
Similarities: 
3 steps in basic figures (SQQ in Foxtrot, 123 in Waltz)
Rise and fall: begin to rise e/o 1s count, continue to rise on 2nd step, sustain the height at beginning of 3rd step, lower at e/o 3rd step
Swing and sway
Different in principals:
Rhythm: 
Waltz (1 2 3) 3 steps to 3 beats (most of the times) - you are allowed to hesitate or syncopate, but the core is 1 2 3 rhythm, with 1 is strongest beat. 
Foxtrot (S QQ) 3 steps to 4 beats of music. QQ are always together (syncopated Q&Q is ok, but still 2 Qs are together). One can different number of Slow's together. In Foxtrot, each step will pass the foot, thus a "walking" dance. 
American Foxtrot (SSQQ) - this goes over a musical phrase (4 beats)
When to release center of gravity when doing swing: 
Waltz Closed Change: "side and slightly fwd" (when a step is straight to the side, it "kills" the momentum.)
Foxtrot Feather and Three Step: "forward and slightly side"
Foxtrot doesn't have as much rise and fall, it is very 'flat' compared to Waltz. Head pretty much stays at the same height. 
This is because of the nature of the movement (passing feet) and the music pace (Foxtrot music is faster than Waltz.)
All rise are in body and some legs. No foot rise. Rise are absorbed in knees. Do not pop up.
Movement principals:
Foot movement: 
Standing foot(leg): the foot/leg you are pushing out of, "displacing yourself out of the standing leg". It could be backward step (pushing off heel) or forward step (pushing out of the ball of the foot).
Free foot could be leading foot (leg) - foot you are arriving into, OR, trailing foot (leg) - foot you just moved out of.
Leading foot: The displacement of center of gravity is causing free foot to move, free foot then moves ahead of the center. It is receiving weight (receiving foot). Leading foot could be going forward or backward.
Trailing foot: the foot drawing in either from back or front (it was previously the standing foot.) It moves after the center.
Foot is not moving on its own. Do not stick foot forward, or backward by itself attempting to achieve bigger steps. 
We keep repeating the process of changing standing foot from one to the other, in a most efficient and visually pleasing way. As weight is leaving (squeezing out of) the standing foot, it is being absorbed immediately into the receiving foot.
Need to use center to achieve bigger movement. Move center and projects the body to move. The harder one squeezes out of standing leg, the further it can project the leading foot. Center, hips are projecting the foot. Foot follows the direction the center is projecting.
Timing with partner is important. 2 sets of feet really moving as 1 set of foot, moving in harmony. 
Effort/power is generated in the first step. 2 step is where power is released and at its highest. 3rd is where everything is gathered and reproduced/regenerated.
To make the movement "milkier", break S into 2 counts, SQQ is 1234, move center on 1 and step on 2 (for the S count). 
Rotation:
Like normal walking: arms are swinging on the side, opposite of the leg. 
We wind up before winding down (rotating), so always starting a little "behind"
In a cycle of 3 steps, there is one continuous rotation to one direction, then switch direction. By the end of 2nd step, you have reached the maximum rotation, e.g. in Three Step, after 2nd step, do not over rotate on 3rd step, just follow through on the step.
The peak of the effort (greatest, fastest) is at the end of the first step. Rotation at maximum by 2nd step. Do not over rotate on the 3rd step.
How much to rotate: 45 degrees on both sides of the traveling direction, total 90 degrees.
Rise and Fall:
It can be argued whether Rise and Fall comes first or rotation - it depends on steps and perspectives.
Music dictates the rise and fall.
Feather step appears to be longer, which is an illusion. Length of Feather and Three Step are not fundamentally different. Feather step looks longer cuz it is outside partner, it rises sooner than Three step
Feather Step: rise from first step, through entire 2nd step and into beginning of 3rd step, lower at the end of 3rd step.
Three Step: it is more compact, there is no rise in first step of Three Step. Rise is delayed till when it's into the second step. 
Swing and Sway
Swing: pendulum swinging, imagine children's swing. It's a movement. Center, leg, foot swings from one high point to low point and to the other high point again.
Any leading side has some elevation of leading hip.
Sway: change of the horizontal tilt. It helps maintain balance and looks more attractive.
"Sway to Left" is when R hip is more up and body is leaning toward Left.
"Sway to Right" is when L hip is more up and body is leaning toward Right.
In Foxtrot, don't think too much about rise and fall, it's all built-in the footwork already. If you do rise, that probably means you are popping up and doing too much rise.
Foxtrot is a curvilinear and rotary dance.
The "straight" figures, such as Feather Step and Three Step, gently curve. On turning figures, either the man is the axis, or the lady.
Very often, frame rotating from one side to another, changing direction after a measure, like monkey swinging from vine to vine. Waltz is more like being on a children's swing "up and down".
Foxtrot is "walking" dance, not a turning dance. It constantly switches between CBM and side leads.
Development of a figure: movement (how to travel in the figure, rise and fall, rotation, sway.
Heel turn in Reverse Turn and Natural Turn, the difference from Waltz:
Waltz: 1st step is already turning, foot is already position for the turn, 
Foxtrot: 1st step has slight curve, but main action is "rolling back", then there is rise which brings foot together, and then turn comes later.
Posture: Elbow is not down, is a bit out, but not as much as Tango. The position is so that you feel the pressure in the hands.
Important to collect before the slow step. Slow is the driving, powerful step. Two quicks are coasting.
When counting "1 2 3 4" (where "S" is "1 2"): On "1" - it's lowering and collecting and delay stepping till "2". Or, one can think of it as "S&", lower on "S" and step on "&".
Head:
Head movement should be smooth. not too fast in Foxtrot. Smooth head movement goes with long stride.
Amount of head movement and body rotation is relative to movement size. If you don't step big, have bigger movement, you shouldn't do a lot of head or rotation. Otherwise, its proportion looks wrong.
Take the time to roll through the feet with control, moving "across" and not rush. Build foot strength to avoid wobbly feet.
On the difference between Natural Turn, Natural Weave, and Hover Cross 
Natural Turn: On second step, hips and frame are square to line of dance. 
Natural Weave: On second step, hips stay square, frame continues to turn to left side leading so lady can step OP on step 4 
Hover Cross: Between step 2 and 3, both hips and frame turn to face center. Then frame stays square to center, while hips turn to step OP wrongside position, and then back to normal OP.
Foxtrot Preparation Step
8 count
1-2-3-4
Man: LF step to L side, hesitate
Lady: RF to R side, show more extension on "4"
5-6-7
Man: RF to R side, preparing to move forward
Lady': LF to L side, keep extension (don't go back to neutral), on "7" L side and L elbow goes even more diagonally leftward for even more extension (but don't go into Man's space), prepare to move RF back. Left heel is down. L leg more straight, but not locked and then move center back and step RF back (from fairly straight L knee position, body is rotated.)
8:
Man: LF forward with slight CBM
Lady: RF back, with slight CBM, roll through RF and push for next step.
Typically, continue with Feather step.

Dance Central - Reverse Pivot
Foxtrot Reverse Pivot (*)
The Foxtrot Reverse Pivot is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
(*) This figure is not listed in the "gray book", but is often danced.
Leader
Begin Backing DC
S or &: RF back in CBMP (small step), LF held in CBMP | BDC| THT | up to 1/2 to L (Pivot) | -- | CBM | --
Finish DW
Pull in ab muscle, focus on turning base. Hip creating the rotation.
Follower
Begin Facing DC
S or &: LF fwd in CBMP (small step), RF held in CBMP | DC | TH | up to 1/2 to L (Pivot) | -- | CBM | --
Finish BDW
Compact small step. Body turns less. Left side positive. Body feels turned to right.

Dance Central - Reverse Turn
Foxtrot Reverse Turn (Feather Finish)
This is a Newcomer figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | FDC | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at e/o 1
Step straight forward DC.
Early rise to lead a heel turn, it's difficult for lady to do heel turn on bent knee, so man needs to rise enough to allow to have straight legs for heel turn. Man "walks with a particularly straight knee" which stops the movement forward and make Lady do heel turn.
Q: RF to side | BDW | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | up
The step is forward relative to the body. There is minimal pivot on the LF. The RF stepping forward relative to the body will thus be stepping between DC and center.The man then can pivot on the RF as he is transferring weight from the LF.
When leading heel turns, brush the foot, it helps to not fall over
Q: LF back | BLOD | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 3
Step straight back.
S: RF back | BLOD | THT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at e/o 4
Q: LF to side, slightly fwd | Pointing DW | T | 3/8 between 4-5, body turns less | Sway(R) | up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDW | TH | - | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 6
S: LF fwd | FDW | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM | -
Follower
S: RF back | BDC | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly 3/o 1, NFR
When feet are traveling backwards, mentally, think about body traveling forward, i.e. shoulder weight does not go back. 
Straight back. drive, big step (bigger steps actually helps with balance, if step is too small, body falls back more easily.)
Keep head even more left which helps to turn.
To initiate the turn, keep shoulders with partner, but turn hips to L, RF is slightly turned in when stepping (foot position always goes with hip position).
R shoulder down, L side stretched, strong diagonal. head follows the elbow.
Do not pop. Absorb rise with knees. Roll weight onto RF. L side positive.
Q: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn) | FLOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | up, NFR.
When dragging LF close to RF, be in sync with Man's leg movement, don't drag too quick.
Do not wait till last second to turn. Body is turning as LF is being dragged back.
Keep left side positive (i.e. body is turned 1/8 to R), do not over turn shoulder. Do not end square with Man. 
Do not pop. Absorb rise with knees.
Heel turn is not on straight knee, legs are slightly bent all the time.
Q: RF fwd | FLOD | TH | - | Sway(R) | up, NFR, lower e/o 3
Push LF off, don't fall short stepping RF forward. Slide ball of feet forward. Settle at the end. Next step starts with heel lead.
Because of the sway to R, L shoulder feels high (because of stretching of L side, but do not raise L shoulder itself.)
S: LF fwd | FLOD | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 4
Lower first, then step, bigger step, straight forward, don't go sideways. Do not turn out foot. Stay very left.
Q: RF to side | BW | TH | 1/4 between 4-5 | Sway(L) | up
Though written as "side step", it is really a swinging "fwd" step, then turn the feet and body. Lower heel, collect before stepping next step in CBMP.
Q: LF back in CBMP | BDW | TH | 1/8 between 5-6, body turns less | Sway(L) | up, NFR, lower e/o 6
Leave left shoulder forward, don't lead back.
S: RF back | BDW | T | - | Sway(S) | CBM | -
Continue with next step, do L toe release.
Note:
Step 4-7 of the Reverse Turn is Feather Finish and may be commenced in other alignments. Less turn may be made.
Feather Finish is the ending of many patterns.
See Heel Turn technique in Standard Technique on Turns.

Foxtrot Silver Routine
Start with one hand hold, Man BDC or BLOD (a bit unusual), stand at the beginning of short side. 
Main with weight on his RF; Lady weight on her LF.
Preparation: one step preparation: Man LF fwd, Lady RF back, head shape to R, make sure to stay on Man's right, do not cross into his space. Picture pose. 
Feather Finish (SQQ): ending Man Facing DW
Three Step (SQQ) 
Hover Cross (SQQQQQQ) at corner, changing LOD. 
Open Telemark and Feather Ending (SQQSQQ)
Hover Telemark (SQQ) 
Natural Weave (SQQ QQ QQ) overturned
S: Man may shape to left, in which case, shape with Man and turn head to left. If Man doesn't shape, keep head to right, but do not get ahead of Man, Man needs to get ahead.
Keep head movement smooth. Scooping action starting S
Overtuned, to end Man Facing LOD.
Reverse Turn (SQQ SQQ)
Lady: For this one, keep head to left, keep classic look.
Last Q: not dropping heel, changing direction to start Top Spin.
Top Spin (QQQQ) ending DW new LOD Make this flatter, make it travel down short side
Reverse Wave (SQQ SQQ)
Man: don't turn too much, flatten it out, more swing on step 5 (Lady is on the outside of turn on this step 5)
Lady: Wave: curve with Man, stay with his right side. slowly turning head back smoothly.
Open Impetus (SQQ) facing DC new LOD
Lady: stay in closed position as long as possible, don't turn head too early.
Weave from PP (SQQ QQ QQ)
Lady: doesn't turn till second step. Follow Man's shape, can shape to L or right.
Three Step (SQQ) Keep straight line, stay in your space.
Natural Telemark (SQQQQ)
Foxtrot Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel and Feather Ending (SQQSQQS) finish in PP in the corner
Lady: Last S: Head can stay in left (in which case, make sure the hip does turn into Promenade position), Or, turn head into Promenade (stay behind Man)
Weave from PP (SQQ QQ QQ)
Repeat from #2.
Foxtrot Gold Routine
Start on short side, Man FDC. 
Preparation (1234 5678) ---------- start short wall

Basic Weave (QQ QQ QQ)
Hover Feather (QQ), extra shaping
Three Step (SQQ) Moving along DW; bigger movement; Lady: 1st Q - swing L hip

Hover Feather (QQ) (For music phrasing)

Reverse Wave (SQQ SQQ) corner

Repeat from Bounce Fallaway

Dance Central - Reverse Wave
Foxtrot Reverse Wave
The Reverse Wave is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1 
Rise at the end.
Q: RF to side | Backing Wall | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up
Q: LF back | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Up. Lower e/o 3
Not a very big step. Slightly different step from normal Reverse Turn because of the following figure (Reverse Wave).
Sway and receive the weight. Next step will be providing the drive.
S: RF back | Backing DW | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Driving step.
Q: LF back | Curving towards LOD | T | 1/8 between 4-6 | Sway(R) | Rise e/o 5
Q : RF back | Backing LOD | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up. Lower e/o 6
S: LF back | Backing LOD | TH | start to turn to R | Sway(S) | CBM | --
S: RF to side small step (Heel Pull) | Facing DC | H, IE of foot, whole foot then IE of LF | 3/8 between 7-8 | Sway(L) | --
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | Body turn to L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Special Note -- Although the rise is taken from the T of LF on step 5, when the RF starts to move back for step 6 the R Heel must be in contact with the floor.
Note -- There are five alignments of the Reverse Wave
Facing LOD 3/8 between 1-3, 1/8 between 4-6, 3/8 between 7-8 (or less at corner)
Facing LOD 3/8 between 1-3, 1/4 between 4-6 to back DW of new LOD, 1/4 between 7-8
Facing DW 1/2 between 1-3, then continue as in alignment (1)
Facing DW 1/2 between 1-3, then continue as in alignment (2)
Facing DC 3/8 between 1-3, 1/4 between 4-6 to back the new LOD, 3/8 between 7-8.
Alignments 2,4, and 5 are used to turn a corner.
Follower
S: RF back | Backing LOD | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
RF back, then keep head there.
Anchor R shoulder down. L side positive. 
Q: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn) | Facing DW | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | continue to rise on 2
Lady heel turn, keep head, turning body underneath, effectively head is now shaped to right. Extend.
Alternatively, the head can keep left/back at previous S, when switching weight to LF at this Q, head draws big arc and change to R. (Action started from foot, ankle, hip to head) (delaying head changes)
Leave shoulder still turned to R, not flat with Man at this time. Like winding up for next move.
Q: RF fwd | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up. Lower e/o 3
Slide fwd more. 
Keep the shape to the R, head is to R. Shoulder rotated to R. L hip fwd/faster.
Enjoy this moment "longer", don't rush changing head to L.
S: LF fwd | Facing DW (moving to wall) | H | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Wave part: Use R leg, range of motion, curve with Man, stay with his right side. slowly turning head back smoothly.
LF fwd, Really push L hip diagonally forward (but not locked), side leading. Head changed to shape to L. 
After LF steps fwd, L shoulder connected to LF. change R shoulder forward at this time.
Q : RF fwd | Curving towards LOD | HT | 1/8 between 4-6 | Sway(L) | Rise e/o 5
Heel lead. Really swing hip to R, to Man, use L leg under butt muscle.
R side body ribs to Man. Head extends more. 
Produce a curve starting from foot, leg and body, coming out from head. Use R leg, bring L leg underneath body. Head curve leftwards. Drive with the leg, not upper body.
Head starting changing to L smoothly. Think about head taking more "space" explore the space. Enjoy the moment longer.
Q: LF fwd | Facing LOD | TH | -- | Sway(L) | Up. Lower e/o 6
Head to L.
S: RF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | --
2nd part of Natural Turn finish. Could do Closed Impetus or Open Impetus here. 
S: LF to side | Backing DC | TH, then IE of T of RF | 3/8 between 7-8 | Sway(R) | --
S: RF back having brushed to LF | Backing DC | T | Body turn to L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Extended Reverse Wave:
Reverse Wave can be extended. Add the following steps SQQ after step 6:
Man: continue BLOD
S: TH, LF back, 
Q: T, RF back, R shoulder leading
Q: TH, LF back in CBMP
Lady: continue facing LOD. Poise is not back weight, but shoulder is dancing in the back of the feet, do not fall forward. 
S: HT, RF fwd, then relax R shoulder down, allow left thigh and knee to dance forward, ahead of body. Drive out of R hip. Swing L hip up and forward for next step
Q: T, LF fwd, L shoulder leading, prepare to step OP. 
Q: TH, RF fwd, CBMP, OP. Pull RF underneath the body. Body is shaping back, which is because center of body is going forward, not bending backward. 
then continue from step 4 again. 
Head: can keep head left, or shape to R. The feeling is legs are dancing ahead of body.

Dance Central - Three Step
Foxtrot Three Step
This is a Newcomer figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | LOD | H
Footwork: two heel lead steps, do not rise on top of toe on this step, keep flat. 
Q: RF fwd | LOD | HT | | Sway(L) | rise e/o 1
Previous "S" is a Heel step (not HT) - do not rise.
slight R side lead (to bring lady back inline after a feather finish)
Slight left side lead
Q: LF fwd | LOD | TH | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 2
Slight left side lead
S: RF fwd | LOD | H | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
(Three Step starts with a S, which is listed as last count of preceding step.)
S: RF back | BLOD | TH
Straight back, no CBM, don't curve to R either. This step has no direction change, it's moving down same one direction. Do not get into Man's space. 
The driving effort is in the first part of the step. Timing of the effort is important to make the step look smooth and together with partner.
At end of the count, lower into the R knee, extend L leg straight back, use RF to really push which will make LF slide back even further for bigger step. 
Throughout the Three Step, both knees are kept flexed, they don't straighten. Do not pop up. Stay flat.
Q: LF back | BLOD | TH | Sway(R) | rise e/o 1, NFR
Make sure feet are on two tracks, leave room for Man to step in between legs. 
Swing L hip, L side back, LF step back (do not step to side, do not turn out LF), think more about extending/straightening left leg which will give Man more room. (Do not allow head to swing to R side of spine. Keep head on left of spine.)
Roll onto LF (with flexed knee). Slight L side lead, toe release. Do not rise up, keep flat with flexed L knee. When doing side lead and rotating to L, it's easy to get into Man's space. Can slide frame a little bit, focus on long stride. Keep very very left. 
Move weight and roll onto LF, while keeping body forward to Man.
Head: could stretch L even more; OR, turn and shape to R, but do not get into Man's space.
Left shoulder/elbow continue to go back and stretch left side, don't stop. It feels like L shoulder is up. It should feel left ear doesn't change position, but because of left side is stretching, the distance between left ear and left shoulder decreases. 
The feeling is chest/left rib cage is more forward to Man, while head and shoulder are extending away (opposition) more, while L knee is bent (soft knee, do not straighten), moving through and extending R leg back. 
Do not rush through QQ. take the time, keep connected with Man (could be hip connection), continuous smooth movement. Man gives more on this Q, Lady extends more to L.
Keep L hip open through the steps. Do not close/square it up. 
Really need to get onto LF, shoulder inline with hip, not just sending L hip back and body becomes displaced. 
It's easy to loose contact in middle during when stepping back. Though movement is going back, the middle/chest should still be very forward to Man. 
Effort is in the first step, not on this Q step. This step is releasing the energy built from 1st step.
This is the peak of the rotation, do not over rotate on next step.
Q: RF back | BLOD | TH | Sway(R) | up, NFR, lower e/o 2
Use abdominal muscle to draw RF back. Do not fall back, do not allow shoulder to go back. Keep blocks of weight. 
Lower into LF with control, RF big step, no stopping, keep continuous movement, do not step on RF and exit too fast, make stepping slow and continuous. Keep the R hip connection with Man so the leg doesn't move too fast back. Keep contact is very key and still stretch L side and extend head to L. Slow down the movement.
No CBM here, straight back. toe release. Keep L shoulder up. 
Do no over rotate, coast the step.
Head pretty much stays where it is from previous step, (do make sure to keep stretching head) and not move into Man's space.
S: LF back | BLOD | T | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Notes
Three step can be danced DW, in which case it could end DW or curved to L to end facing LOD.
CBM happens gradually across the three steps, so the frame wound to the left by the end of the figure.
Even when moving backward, Lady's body (R rib cage, center, left side as well) is always forward to Man.

Dance Central - Basic Weave
Foxtrot Basic Weave
This is a Bronze figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Q: LF fwd | DC Against LOD | HT | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at e/o 1
Q: RF to side | Backing LOD | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up 
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | T | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Up
The footwork of TH may be used. This will often result in a softer and more flowing movement. Rise on 4 will then be "Up, NFR"
Q: RF back | Backing DC | T | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | T | 1/4 between 4-5 | Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up, lower e/o 6 
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
Q: RF back | Backing DC Against LOD | TH | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly end of 1, NFR
Make sure belly button/lower center is constantly toward partner.
Q: LF to side | Pointing DC | T | 1/4 L between 1-2 | Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
If Man shapes, Lady turns her head to R
Leave head and only turn body to L, which effectively make head on the R side.
Keep head flat, knees will absorb foot rise. Do not pop up. 
During Weave, knees and thighs are ahead of body. Do not take head too fast and past feet. Relax R shoulder down. Take knees across standing leg when moving forward. 
Use leg to push, use butt muscle to move more, not sitting. Activate the muscle, but not like Samba tick, don't flat the hip crease. When using muscle to push and move more, it makes it easier to extend and shape upper body more too.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | T | -- | Sway(R) | Up
Traveling down LOD at this moment. Left shoulder is quite forward at this point.
Don't fall into the step, use L leg to push.
To create more volume, rotate hip to R, L shoulder more forward, shape diagonal forward (not down), extend head to create the shape. (Do not just bend back upper body to crate shape.)
Q: LF fwd | Facing DC | T | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Extended Weave: step 3 and 4 can be repeated many "QQ"s - side leading, lady project fwd left side. Don't peel off from Man. Center to Man. When Man starts to collect, that indicates finishing, but still Lady should do Toe leads in next step. Though written as "T", instructor says do TH to drive the next step.
Do not get into Man's space. -- very common error.
Keep left connected to Man, do not slide connection, do not step to side, step straight forward (there is CBM, but don't step "cross middle line".)
L shoulder/head/spine/hip connect down to LF so you don't get pulled backward.
Q: RF to side | Backing Wall | TH | 1/8 L between 4-5 | Sway(L) | Up
Swinging R leg, keep head left.
Point fwd, as transferring weight to RF turn foot and head to left. body turns less, leave upper body to Man to prepare next OP step.
Turn head back to left.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 L between 5-6 | Body turns less | Sway(L) | Up NFR Lower e/o 6
S: RF back | Backing DW | T | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Note:
More advanced Lady dancers may, if the wish, use ball of foot to whole foot on the step preceding this pattern.
Basic Weave can be used along LOD, but often used at corner.
When Man shapes, lady turn head as shown above.
The figure is flat, do not rise. Use knees to absorb foot rise.
Connecting with Reverse Turn:
After SQQ of Reverse Turn, ending Man BDW, do a check step on "S":
Man needs to step back with RF, shape to L, raising his L arm, small step
Lady could do either toe lead or heel lead, depending on what kind of effect Man wants to have
Man could collect first, then take back step, in which case, it would be heel lead for Lady.
If Man doesn't lower to collect, then Lady takes a toe lead.
Lady collecting LF to RF (toe dragging on floor) and then step fwd HT (or TH depending on Man), shape to L, raising R side, really extend L side, head extends to L as far as possible.
Then continue with Basic Weave.
Alignment:
Could be danced as shown above, 1-4 Reverse Turn will end BDW, movement is along LOD
OR, danced at corner to change LOD (more often used): 1-4 Reverse Turn to end BLOD near corner, check and Basic Weave turning one of the following to end DW of new LOD
1/8, 1/8, 3/8
1/4, 1/8, 3/4

Dance Central - Natural Pivot Turn
Quickstep Natural Pivot Turn
The Quickstep Natural Pivot Turn is Newcomer Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
This could start in closed position or outside partner position, depending on the previous step.
Leader
S: RF fwd | FDW | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1
Q: LF side | BDC | T | 1/4 to R between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF closes to LF | BLOD | TH | 1/8 to R between 2-3 | Sway(R) | Up, lower e/o 3
collect at e/o 3
S: LF back (RF held in CBMP) | Point BDW | THT | R up to 1/2 turn | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Heel is in contact with floor while turn is made on ball of foot. On next step, RF should be on same track as LF. Place down heel at end of pivot, (do not transfer weight to heel too early - that will cut the turn short and you won't be able to complete the turn) and then up on ball to drive into next step. 
Follower
S: LF back | BDW | TH | starts to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1, NFR
Curve to left, make sure to still give left side forward to Man, keep head very left, really extend. Don't fall back.
Continue moving center with Man and leave head behind to create the shape.
Q: RF side | PLOD | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | body turns less | Sway(L) | Up
Make sure to get out of Man's way, RF moving direction is not down LOD, it's BDW, foot is pointing LOD. 
Follow Man's hip to gauge RF step size. If too big, Lady will get in front of Man. Lady is inside of the turn.
Q: LF close to RF | FLOD | TH | body completes turn | Sway(L) | Up, lower e/o 3
Not as much rise, knee stay flexed, There is Sway and Shape to L, but much faster. Lower into legs/knees faster. "drop faster". Use the power on movement. 
S: RF fwd (pivot) | FLOD | HTH | (Heel is kept in contact with floor) | up to 1/2 to R | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Heel is in contact with floor, though footwork is HTH.
Keep upper thigh contact with partner (R to R leg) for all pivot and spin turns.
Keep strong elbow, stay to left, elbow turn, leg just follow elbow.
Stay low, don't rise.
LF is not held in CBMP for lady, but CBM will be used on next step except when Natural Spin Turn follows the pivot.
Keep feet on two tracks at end of this step.
After pivot, posture should be still good, upper body should not be forward. R hip forward, don't stick butt out. It needs to finish with full weight on RF before pushing RF to drive to next step. Don't fall into next step.
There is a tendency for body to lean forward and move to R. (Try not turning head to R. Turning head to R tends to make body shift to R, which is not good.) Stay very left (Man's R side).
Shaping: often this shapes to R. Head to R.
Notes:
Difference between a Pivot Turn and a Spin Turn is that there is no Rise in Pivot Turn. There is Rise (and brush) in Spin Turn.
At corner: turn 3/8 to face DW on new LOD.
Turn amount is up to 1/2 to face BLOD, followed by any Natural figure.

Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending
Foxtrot Fallaway with Weave Ending

This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | HT | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
&: RF back in Fallaway, R side leading | Backing DW, moving down LOD | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 2
Slight flexing of the knees; heel lowers towards floor and may lightly touch
Q: LF back in CBMP and Fallaway | Backing DW, moving down LOD | T | No turn | Sway(S)| Down. Rise e/o 3
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing DW | T | - | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 4
Slight flexing of the knees; heel lowers towards floor and may lightly touch
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 4-5 | Sway(S) | | Down. Rise e/o 5
QQQS: 4-7 of overturned Basic Weave | T T TH T | Sway(SRRS) | CBM on step 6 and 9
Follower
S: RF back | Backing DC | THT | - | Sway(S) | Rise e/o 1
Lady's head is to left at this time. Keep previous head shape (maybe think about doing more).
THT footwork: There is a bounce/rise action. dancing the last T footwork is to dance body forward to get onto Toe of RF. It creates lightness through the legs.
Release L leg faster.
&: LF back in Fallaway, L side leading | Backing DC, moving down LOD | T | - | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 2
LF bigger step, more like a side step LOD. follow Man's hips. Weight onto L leg. Don't over turn shoulders or turn shoulders early. Shoulder is flat, going into Fallaway.
Stay very left, don't get in front of Man. 
It feels like Man stays in the fallaway longer (compared to Fallaway and Slip Pivot).
Default is a reverse weave action, if leader wants to do fallaway, he will block at the hips, which makes lady open immediately and do a Fallaway.
Turn head into PP position. Keep volume/space with Man (think about even more shape).
Use glute hip more forward to Man, shoulder toward Man (helps to create the PP shape), do not open up side.
Q: RF back in CBMP and Fallaway | Backing DC, moving down LOD | T | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Down. Rise e/o 3
Keep head to right. 
Keep body to Man, very stretched. Keep head volume with Man.
Cushion the knees
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing to LOD | T | 3/8 between 3-4, body turns less | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 4
Swivel RF, not turning body and then step LF fwd. L side positive. Keep shoulder parallel to Man.
Feels like left shoulder is leading and stretching forward
The energy has to keep very leftward (stretch L side) and not get into Man's space (when head is to R, easy to get into Man's space)
Head options:
can keep head to R all the way till feather finish. Don't rush turning head, smooth movement. stretch to L.
Or at this time, take the time to turn head back to left, articulate the head.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing LOD | T | Slight body turn | Sway(S) | | Down. Rise e/o 5
Body diagonally stretched.
QQQS: 4-7 overturned Basic Weave | T TH TH T | | Sway(SLLS) | CBM on step 6 and 9
QQ: swing R side, and keep side leading position. Do not finish square and flat to Man. 
Changing angle from right to left, spine and head to left
Head turns back to left when RF to side/back closing up with Man.
Release leg at very end to give room to Man to step.

Foxtrot Change of Direction
This is a Bronze figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | H | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
At the preceding step (e.g. feather finish), frame should already be turning.
This step has CBM, with LF turned out a bit.
S: RF diag fwd R side leading, and close LF to RF slightly fwd without weight | DW R Toe pointing towards LOD, End facing DC | IE of TH, then IE of T of LF | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | --
At a corner, 3/8 or 1/2 turn may be made.
Pay attention to move along the same line/direction, do not change moving direction by doing step side - it's a fwd step. (though some coaches say this is a side step, don't do forward step.)
Big step, push off LF.
S: LF fwd in CBMP | Facing DC | H | Sway(S) | CBM | --
This is exactly like a preparation step, shaping to right
Follower
S: RF back | Backing DW | TH | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Head really turns to L, follow the L elbow. Collect LF and push off RF to side.
S: LF diag, back, L side leading, and close RF to LF slightly back without weight | Backing DW, End backing DC | IE of TH, then IE of T of RF | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | --.
Upper body stay with partner. do not get into his space.
Pull weight onto LF, with balance. Do not anticipate movement. Allow L hip to finish, then keep turning L. 
S: RF back in CBMP | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Option: This is like preparation step, shaping to left.
Depending on next, this could shape to R, head turn to R.

Dance Central - Dance Practice
Structured Practice Plan
To make progress, one has to have good practice on the technique. The idea of practice is to get the repetitions into muscle memory. 
Everyone is different, here are some practice ideas, but it's important to make a plan that works you.
Decide on how much time each week will be for practice. e.g. whether it's 4-5 days a week, 1-2 hours per day, or 1-2 times a week, have a plan.
Decide on the 2 to 3 dances to practice on each day. It may take more than a week to rotate through all dances, which is ok.
Decide on the 2 to 3 elements/technique to improve on each dance for the next few weeks or months.
At Practice Time
Warm up: do exercises to get body warmed up. Play a couple of songs, e.g. Samba music for rib cage exercise. Songs are time keeper too.
Practice the basic actions for the dances to practice that day. See Latin exercises and Standard exercises.
For each of the targeted element/technique: practice ONE at a time for 15-30 min, then switch
When it's near performance or competition time, run through routines.
Cool down by doing stretches at the end.
Notes for practice:
When practicing alone, pretend there is a partner, hold your frame.
Practice slowly first, then with music.

Dance Central - Top Spin
Foxtrot Top Spin
The Top Spin is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing Against LOD | T | 1/8 to L between preceding step and 1 | Sway(S) | Up
Preceding step is generally a Feather Finish. To continue with Top Spin (to avoid traffic for example), the previous Feather Finish is cut short so you can respond quickly into Top Spin action. 
"Backing Against LOD" means Man is facing LOD but your next step will be stepping backward (against LOD). This is different from BLOD, which is "Backing LOD" your next step is down LOD.
Start turning left hip to left. This is not a large step. Then use CBM. 
Q: RF back | Backing DW Against LOD | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | CBM | UP
Connect right shoulder down to right side.
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DC of new LOD | T | 1/4 between 2-3. body turns less | Sway(R) | UP
Swing left hip forward.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Lower e/o 4
See Note for other alignments. Could finish DW of new LOD.
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
Q: RF fwd in CBMP OP | Facing Against LOD | T | 1/8 to L between preceding step and 1 | Sway(S) | Up
When Man cut short Feather Finish, Lady respond to keep weight on T (at end of Feather Finish).
Follow Man's body, do not be too powerful, the figure is compact. Mainly used in corners or get out of trouble. Man can change alignments. 
"Facing Against LOD": think of typical Feather Finish ending, you'll be changing direction, so you'll be stepping forward (against LOD).
Q: LF fwd | Facing DW Against LOD | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Monitor through Man's central line, keep arm light.
Connect head, shoulder hip to left foot.
Q: RF to side | Backing new LOD | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Up
Allow feet to swivel, feet turns more than body
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | TH | 1/8 between 3-4, body turns less | Sway(L) | Up. NFR Lower e/o 4
Think about stretching diagonally to the right, rather than rotation in upper body.
S: RF back | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Notes
On step 1 the footwork of TH may be used. This will often result in a softer and more flowing movement. When this footwork is used the rise will be "Up NFR" on step 2. 
On step 6 of the preceding Reverse Turn with Feather Ending, Man's body will be facing DW and the Lady will be backing DW.
Silver and Gold: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish may be danced turning 1/2 on step 1-3 to back against LOD and 3/8 on Feather Finish to face DC against LOD. Continue with Top Spin to face DW.
Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel may be danced, turning only 1/8 to R for Man, 3/8 for Lady on step 7 to follow with Feather Ending DC against LOD into Top Spin.

Dance Central - Weave From PP
Foxtrot Weave from Promenade Position
The Weave from Promenade Position is a Silver Level figure. Leader
S: RF fwd in PP and CBMP | Pointing DC Body facing LOD | HT | Sway(S) | rise e/o 1
feet, low center, high center: the offset position of these 3 points must be maintained through PP and weave. 
Q: LF fwd | Facing DC | T | start to turn L | Sways(S) | CBM | Up
Angle left shoulder and hip down, do not pull weight back.
Step through a curved line.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing DW | T | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Up
Energy still moves diagonal center. Do not think about BLOD.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 3-4 | Sway(L) | Up
Pull center back
Q: RF back | Backing LOD | T | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Connect R shoulder and hip down, Man is inside of turn.
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | T | 3/8 between 5-6 Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up

Alternate alignments for the Weave from PP may be found in the Waltz charts.
Follower
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Pointing to center (moving DC) | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1
Keep center toward the Man.
Body doesn't turn till second step.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing DC | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | Up
Depends on how Man shapes. Head can keep left or right. Head following the arc.
If shaping to R, keep head position, turn body underneath (base rotate), effectively, head will shape to right during weave. 
Think step forward and it will end in "side and slightly back"
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing to LOD | T | 3/8 between 2-3 Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
Keep knee soft, do not stick tummy to man, pull center back.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing LOD | T | -- | Sway(R) | Up
Q : LF fwd | Facing LOD | T | start to turn L | Sways(S) | CBM | Up. 
R side is the driving side, left side is swinging fwd. The driving side is always the lower side, not the swinging side.
Q: RF to side | Backing Wall | TH | 1/4 between 5-6 | Sway(L) | Up
Closing head to left.
Swivel the feet. Feet turns more.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 between 6-7 Body turns less | Sway(L) | Up. NFR Lower e/o 7
S: RF back | Backing DW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --

Quickstep Natural Spin Turn
The Quickstep Natural Spin Turn is a Newcomer Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
The footwork is the same as Waltz Natural Spin Turn. Timing: SQQSSS.
Leader
S: RF fwd | FDW | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise at end of 1
Q: LF to side | BDC | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Cont to rise on 2 and 3 
Rise on toes, it will help to bring the feet together on 3rd step.
Have continuous swing of the leg through the step.
Q: RF closes to LF | BLOD | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(R) | Lower at end of 3
S: LF back | BLOD, toe turned in | THT | 1/2 to R (pivot) | Sway(S) | CBM | No rise.
S: RF fwd in CBMP | FLOD | HT | Cont to turn | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 5
S: LF to side and slightly back | BDC | TH | 3/8 between 5-6 | Sway(S) | Up, Lower e/o 6
Follower
S: LF back | BDW | TH | Starts to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 1, NFR
Leave head behind, turn body. Continue to keep head to left during the whole Natural Spin Turn.
Q: RF to side | PLOD | T | 3/8 between 1-2, body turns less | Sway(L) | Cont to rise on 2 and 3
R side stretched, shape/sway to L.
Q: LF closes to RF | FLOD | TH | body completes turn | Sway(L) | Lower e/o 3
S: RF fwd | FLOD | HT | 1/2 to R (pivot) | Sway(S) | CBM | No rise
Keep head to far left, follow L elbow, outside circle. Extend.
S: LF back and slightly side | BLOD | T | Cont to turn | Sway(S) | Rise e/o 5
Collect center back to the spine.
S: RF diagonally fwd after brushing to LF | FDC | TH | 3/8 between 5-6 | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 6
Have the middle of body go forward to Man and keep leg moving, but leaving head behind which creates more extension, then head catches up. Don't pause body movement. Simply just leave head behind.
When connecting with V6 (QQS lock step), the footwork for this count becomes THT.

Curved Feather from Promenade Position
Foxtrot Curved Feather from Promenade Position (*)
The Foxtrot Curved Feather from Promenade Position is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
(*) Not listed in "gray book", but often danced.
Leader
S: RF fwd across CBMP in Promenade Position | DW | HT | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Start in Promenade Position
Q: LF diag fwd, preparing to step OP, L side leading | Wall | T | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Against LOD | TH | Sway(R) | CBM | Up. Lower e/o 3
Follower
S: LF fwd in CBMP in PP | Facing DC | HT | Sway(S) | CBM
Stay behind Man's hip. Poised upward position. Do not anticipate the curve.
Man will slowly bring across.
Q: RF to side slightly back, R side leading | LOD | T | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | 
Use left leg and hip to push to right. sending arm across the body.
Very strong upward curve for right side.
Stretching does not mean turning shoulder to right. It's R side upward stretch. 
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing against LOD | TH | Sway(L) | CBM
Small step.
Do not allow stomach to relax. R side lengthen.
Keep individual balance.
Ending facing LOD.

Dance Central - Natural Turn
Quickstep Natural Turn
The Quickstep Natural Turn is a Newcomer Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
This could start in closed position or outside partner position, depending on the previous step.
Leader
S: RF fwd | FDW | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1
Do not lean on Lady, keep R shoulder flat.
Q: LF side | BDC | T | 1/4 to R between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF closes to LF | BLOD | TH | 1/8 to R between 2-3 | Sway(R) | Up, lower e/o 3
Collect at e/o 3
S: LF back | BLOD | TH | cont to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | - 
S: RF to side, small step (heel pull) | FDW of new LOD | H, IE of foot, WF | 3/8 between 4-5 | Sway(S) | --
The heel pull can be underturned to face new LOD.
S: LF fwd | FDW | H | -- | Sway(S) | slight CBM | --
Follower
S: LF back | BDW | TH | starts to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1, NFR
Pay special attention not to tilt shoulder. Shoulder needs to maintain flat throughout. Head follows elbow.
When stepping back, roll from toe to heel, gives you more distance, bigger step, so body doesn't go further than feet.
Use CBM, body and feet are already turning slightly.
When preceding step finishes with head turned to R, on this S, do not turn head sharp to left. Leave head and turn body underneath, smooth movement.
Q: RF side | PLOD | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | body turns less | Sway(L) | Up
Make sure to clear space for Man to pass and not block him.
Q: LF close to RF | FLOD | TH | body completes turn | Sway(L) | Up, lower e/o 3
S: RF fwd | FLOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Big driving step.
S: LF to side | Back new LOD | TH | 1/4 between 4-5 | Sway(S) | --
Technically it's a side step. But in practice, it's often a fwd step, then turn.
Do not step too early, wait till Man finishes his heel turn part and and step/place weight as the Man does the "pull" part.
S: RF back | BDW | T | 1/8 between 5-6 | Sway(S) | slight | --
Collect RF first before stepping back. Big step.
Notes:
The step can be underturned to end Man facing new LOD, in which case, 1/4 turn should be completed between 4-5.

Closed Impetus And Feather Finish
Foxtrot Closed Impetus and Feather Finish
This is a Newcomer figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF back | BLOD | TH | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | - 
Step straight back, not to the side (which will it harder for Lady to catch up).
Q: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | FDC | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | rise e/o 2
Rise up balanced on step 2
Q: LF to side and slightly back | Backing DC Against LOD | TH | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 3
Push off foot on step 3. 
S : RF back | Backing DC Against LOD | THT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 4
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | PDC | T | 1/4 between 4-5, body turns less | Sway(R) | up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDC | TH | - | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 6
Correct footwork is: pointing right toe forward, touching floor with outside edge of toe, and sliding that toe forward as Man pushes off his left foot.
Do not do heel lead.
S: LF fwd | FDC | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM | -
Follower
S: RF fwd | FLOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | -
To get around Man (a lot of distance to cover), lower first, then step with CBM, take LF with it, but don't step LF too early.
Straighten the R leg (smoothly); do not bent R knee when R heel hit ground.
Q: LF to side | BDC | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | rise e/o 2
Step LF to side, late - till after body is turned. The whole motion should be a smooth motion.
Get onto LF first, before articulating head. Keep right side very forward to Man in PP. 
Collect center (belly button) to draw RF
Q: RF diag fwd having brushed to LF | Facing DC Against LOD | TH | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 3
Really push off LF. (body should be on Man's R side)
Head can optionally turn to R if Man does the shaping.
S: LF fwd | Facing DC Against LOD | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 4
Lower first, then step. Make sure upper body does not go into Man first and then shape back out (it's often unconsciously done.) Keep upper body shape.
Q: RF to side | BLOD | TH | 1/8 between 4-5 | Sway(L) | up
Don't take arm back. Arm "breathes", forward to Man. Head changes back to left (if it was previously turned to R.)
Q: LF back to CBMP | BDC | TH | 1/8 between 5-6 , body turns less | Sway(L) | up, NFR lower e/o 6
Left shoulder forward. 
S: RF back | BDC | T | Sway(S) | CBM

Dance Central - Quickstep Technique
See Also: Standard Technique page for general technique for International standard dances.
Quickstep
Closed syllabus level figures have no PP in Quickstep. Keep head closed most of the time. Head change has to be result of something else 
The rise and swing in Quickstep is less than in Waltz, as there isn't time for the full swing and rise of Waltz.
When it's syncopated chasse, Q&Q, it is flat, absorb rise in knees and ankles. When moving, it may appear to be going downward because of stepping, but do not think about going down, think about keeping flat. 
Lady: dance with soft knees, track Man's right thigh.
How does lady know if a step is a lock step? 
QQ timing, step is elevated, and moving forward and back (as opposed to side-ways).
Don't rush the Slow's.

Dance Central - Tumble Turn
Tumble Turn
The Foxtrot Tumble Turn is an Open Level figure. It essentially consists of a Feather Finish with a Reverse Pivot. Timing is SQ&Q. 
Lady's head starts right, turns to left over first three steps, then turns right on Pivot.

Q: RF crosses behind LF | Facing LOD | T | Sway(S)

Dance Central - Closed Telemark
Foxtrot Closed Telemark
The Closed Telemark is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Technical details for Foxtrot Telemark is similar to Waltz Closed Telemark. Foxtrot timing is SQQS, as shown below.
Leader
S: LF fwd | FDC | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1
Compress right side.
Q: RF to side | almost BLOD | T | slightly less than 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up
Connect left shoulder and hip down. Swing R leg ahead of body.
Strong base swivel action. For Closed Telemark, body turns a bit more equally, turn Lady back to closed position. Body comes out in same alignment as feet.
In Open Telemark, isolate base relative to the turn of upper body.
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | TH | slightly over 3/8 between 2-3. body turns less | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 3
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | DW | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
S: RF back | BDC | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Connect right shoulder down, do not turn shoulder. Present left side up in diagonal line.
Strong upward feeling.
Whichever direction you are turning, it's the opposite shoulder that will go down.
Q: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn) | LOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | continue to rise on 2
Dance right hip across right foot, feel left side lengthen, then step RF out..
Q: RF to side and slightly back | BDW | TH | 3/8 between 2-3, body turns less | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 3
Keep body left.
S: LF back in CBMP | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
This is a closed finish.

Curved Feather to Back Feather
Foxtrot Curved Feather to Back Feather
The Foxtrot Curved Feather to Back Feather is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Q: LF diag fwd, preparing to step OP, L side leading | Facing DW | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing wall | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | R | CBM | Up. Lower e/o 3
S: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | TH | 1.8 between 3-4 | S | CBM | Start to rise e/o 4, NFR
Gradual rise is used in Back Feather.
Q: RF back R side leading | Curving toward LOD | T | - | Sway (L) | | Continue to rise on 5
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing LOD | TH | 1/8 between 4-6 | Sway (L) | | Up. Lower e/o 6.
S: RF back | Backing LOD | T | - | Sway (S) | CBM | - | 
Follower
S: LF back | Backing LOD | TH | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1, NFR
Curve, the rotation is mostly in the hip area, follow Man's hip, don't stop turning to R (other you'll block Man), continue the rotation and give Man middle and side and stay connected. 
Upper body/shoulder should not turn too much which will pull off from partner (but it does turn to R, otherwise Man can't pass). Upper body is diagonally stretched (R side is upward), keep parallel to partner.
Push off standing leg to move. 
Forward with body and arms.
Q: RF back, R side leading | Backing DW | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up
Follow leader's direction - there maybe feeling of a forward step, then body turns.
Hip turns more with Man, body turns less (wind up, shoulder should not be flat with hip), body prepares to swing for next step. Shoulder keep parallel with Man. 
Lady can lower her R heel on step 2.
Head to L.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing Wall | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | CBM | Up. Lower e/o 3
Small step LF behind RF, shape to L, like winding up to go. Lady should lower late on 3.
Hip and body rotating to R, follow Man's hips, rotate hip to Man, connected with Man, hip is still in front of Man, not to the side of Man's hip.
Leave RF loosely in front, do not stick R leg too early.
Prepare to really use LF to push for next RF fwd step. This is the circling movement action, get weight into heel and then push forward.
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | HT | 1/8 between 3-4 | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 4
Rotate hip more and lower more into L knee, push off LF/L leg to drive for bigger movement. 
Do no t going downward in this step. The lowering happens in previous step (weight into heel on previous Q). This steps drives forward and up (rise e/o this step).
Left side now swings forward (body does not stop - which will make it look like going backward), do not swing too much up, focus on forward, body rotate a lot to the right. 
Head turns and shapes to R. Do not cross into Man's space. Smooth changes. 
Movement should feel "free", not constrained. Body breathes.
Q: LF fwd L side leading | Curving towards LOD | T | - | Sway(R) | Continue to rise on 5
If Man shapes, Lady turn her head to R, but stay very left, keep ribs/side to Man.
Really needs to turn body (rotate to R)
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing LOD | TH | 1/8 between 4-6 | Sway(R) | Up, Lower e/o 6
S: LF fwd | Facing LOD | H | - | S | CBM | - 
Typically, continue with Feather Finish SQQ. 
This first LF fwd on S, do not follow Man's curve, step straight forward in between Man's legs, otherwise, at the end of Feather finish, Lady's hip will gap/end up to the side of Man's hip. 
When swing on Q: do not dip shoulder low, keep should flat, swing forward, traveling. Give center to Man, shape. 
Breathe, release so body does not stiff up.
if Man shapes, Lady turn her head to R on QQ, keep ribs to Man.

Leader

Q: LF side | BDC | T | 1/4 to R between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Up

S: LF closes to RF without weight | DC | ID of T (LF) | -- | Sway(L) | --

S: LF back | BDW | TH | starts to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1, NFR

S: RF fwd | FLOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | --

Could underturn, only 1/4 to face new LOD.
Always, always stay on Man's right side.
No rise in legs, just extend left side, the rise is in the body
S: RF closes to LF without weight | BDC | IE of T (RF) | - | Sway(R) | --

Foxtrot Curved Three Step (*)
Foxtrot Curved Three Step is Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.

Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing LOD | HT 
From previous step, compress into R hip/shoulder. Use range of motion to signal lady that she's turning to left, sending her to inside of turn.
After stepping onto LF, L shoulder is slightly lower than the R, shape through diagonal (Sway to L) 
Q: RF fwd, R shoulder leading | Center | T | Total 1/2 turn to L between 2-3 | Sway(L) | rise e/o 1
Total 3 steps to make 1/2 turn for the figure. Make the arc as smooth as possible. More rotation, not traveling as much.
Continue to stretch through the diagonal.
Q: LF fwd | Facing Against LOD | TH | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 2
Small step fwd and then compress into L knee.
Follower
S: RF back | BLOD | TH | Sway(R) | rise e/o 1, NFR
More rotation, travels less. T
Follow the Man with your center. Lower R shoulder to R hip.
Avoid turning shoulder to L, which pulls away from Man and cross his tie line.
Q: LF back, L shoulder leading | Wall | T | 1/2 turn to L between 2-3 | Sway(R) | up, NFR, lower e/o 2
With shaping, head can open to R. But pay attention to not cross the tie line. (Can keep head L as well.)
Q: RF back | Backing Against LOD | TH | Sway(S) | CBM | --

Can be followed with Feather Finish.

Leader or Follower

Q: RF BW | Backing DW | T | -- | -- | Continue to rise on 2 and 3
Q: LF crosses in front of RF | Backing DW | T| -- | --
S: RF diag and back | Backing DW | TH | -- | -- | Up. Lower e/o 4

Body should be backing between wall and DW throughout.
Lock step has zero turn, more rotation. Rotate more to R.

Dance Central - Passing Natural Turn
Quickstep Passing Natural Turn
The Quickstep Passing Natural is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd, CBMP in PP | Pointing DW, Body Facing Wall | HT
Starting in Promenade Position.
Q: LF to side, slightly back | BLOD | T | turning to R
Drive off R leg, L leg swing ahead of body, allow diagonal stretch
Q: RF back, R shoulder leading | BLOD | TH
Man changing track from lane 1 (in step 1) to lane 3 (step 3)
Follower
S: LF across fwd, in CBMP, in PP | Moving DW, pointing LOD | HT
Starting in Promenade Position.
Q: RF fwd small step, in between partner's feet | LOD | T
Stay on Man's R side when he changes lanes. Small step, do not block Man. 
Swing R side. Body dance to the right, leave head behind, keep head weight left, do not let head weight join R side of body.
Q: LF fwd, L shoulder leading, prepare for stepping OP in CBMP | LOD | TH
Note: This figure is not in the "book", but is often danced.

Quickstep Progressive Chasse, Starting BDC
Leader
S: RF back | BDC | TH | start to turn L | - | CBM | start to rise e/o 1, NFR
Q: LF to side | PDW | T | 1/4 between 1-2, body turns less | - | cont to rise on 2 and 3
Keep lady in front, do not end up side by side. 
Q: RF closes to LF | DW | T | slight body turn | -- | -- | No rise & fall
S: LF to side/slightly fwd | DW | TH | -- | -- | Up, lower e/o 4
Next step, starts with CBM, OP
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | DW | H | -- | -- | CBM
Follower
S: LF fwd | FDC | HT | start to turn L | -- | CBM | start to rise e/o 1
Q: RF to side | BW | T | 1/8 to L between 1-2 | -- | cont to rise on 2 and 3
Direct center at Man. 
Do not allow shoulder to come up. Keep shoulders down. 
Q: LF closes to RF | BDW | T | 1/8 between 2-3, body turns less | -- | -- | No rise & fall
Hip rotate to L, keeping direction of shoulder
S: RF to side slightly back | BDW | TH | -- | -- | Up, lower e/o 4
Next step, starts with CBM. Do not touch right heel to ground until left heel is even with right foot.
Ending OP, left side lifted, right side rotated to R/back. Upper body twisted to R, keep upper body towards the Man. 
S: LF back in CBMP | BDW | T | -- | -- | CBM
Outside partner position.

Leader
S: RF back | BLOD | TH | start to turn L | -- | CBM | start to rise e/o 1, NFR
Q: LF to side | PDW | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | body turns less | -- | cont to rise on 2 and 3
Q: RF closes to LF | DW | T | slight body turn | -- | --
S: LF to side/slightly fwd | DW | TH | -- | -- | Up, lower e/o 4
position of body should be facing between wall and DW.
Follower
S: LF fwd | LOD | HT | start to turn L | -- | CBM | start to rise e/o 1
Q: RF to side | W | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | -- | cont to rise on 2 and 3
Q: LF closes to RF | BDW | T | 1/8 between 2-3, body turns less | -- | --
S: RF to side slightly back | BDW | TH | -- | Up, lower e/o 4
