Dance Central - Turning Lock
Waltz Turning Lock
The Waltz Turning Lock is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Count 1 (1/2 beat): RF back, R side leading | BDC | T | -- | Sway(L) | start to rise e/o 1
Thigh and hips are 1/8 to R. (not turning feet)
Angle through diagonal, head stretch leftward. Poised diagonally forward. Do not pull back.
Count & (1/2 beat): LF crosses in front of RF | BDC | T | -- | Sway(L) | rise on 2 and 3
Count 2: RF back and slightly R | BDC | T | start to turn L | Sway(S) | --
Rotate through the hips, pivot RF and turn, make sure Lady stays with you.
Count 3: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | TH | 1/4 btwn 3-4, body turns less | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 4
Count 1: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDW | H | -- | Sways(S) | CBM | --
Follower
Count 1 (1/2 beat): LF fwd, L side leading | DC | T | -- | Sway(R) | start to rise e/o 1
After end of previous step (often, Natural Spin Turn) which is ending R side lead, connect R shoulder down. Really swing L side forward, let L hip and L side come diagonally forward (feels like shoulder rotating to R) sooner, changing it to L side lead. Not simply sticking LF fwd, the left side has to come through. 
L side leading, but left is not the driving side. The driving side is actually from standing leg and right lat muscle at end of previous step.
Man starts opening up body and shaping to R, leading Lady to turn body/head to R
Don't think about dancing around the Man, think about dancing "through" the Man.
Keep all step size in sync with Man.
Middle of body stays with Man, keep contact and fill in the space
Count & (1/2 beat): RF crosses behind LF | DC | T | -- | Sway(R) | rise on 2 and 3
Glute forward, don't sag. head weight to left.
Both knees are flexed, cushioned. Both feet on toes, soft. Make sure weight is over ball of RF, soft, cushioned. Use the ankle and knees to push out.
Count 2: LF fwd and slightly L | DC | T | start to turn L | Sway(S) 
Be very ab aware when rising, pull center back and stay on your own balance.
Body dancing forward, arms to partner.
Count 3: RF to side and slightly back | BDW | TH | 1/4 btwn 3-4, body turns less | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 4
This is closed finish. 
This could end in PP (after RF brushes to LF) in which case, head shape to right in its space. Do not turn head too fast or too early.
Count 1: LF back in CBMP | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Next step, Man will step OP.
Note:
Turning lock curves gently forward,
In Turning Lock, foot rise comes before body rise (rise on count 2 LF, or end of 1& on RF).
Last step of preceding Natural Spin Turn:
Footwork for will be the normal TH for Man and Lady.
It may be shaped to the man's left, leading the lady to turn her head right.
The Turning Lock may end in PP, see Outside Change ended in PP.

Open Impetus and Cross Hesitation
Waltz Open Impetus and Cross Hesitation
The Waltz Open Impetus and Cross Hesitation is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Count 1: LF back | BLOD | TH | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | - 
This is "Open Impetus".
Main the sway from previous Natural Turn and transition into PP. See Open Impetus to Wing Notes 
Count 2: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | FDC | HT | 3/8 btwn 1-2 | Sway(L) | rise e/o 2 
Count 3: LF diag. fwd in PP, L side leading | Pointing DC, body facing LOD | TH | slight body turn to R | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 3
Think about a diagonal line to come out in PP.
Count 1: RF fwd in PP and CBMP | Pointing DC, body facing LOD | HT | -- | Sway(S) | rise e/o 4
This is "Cross Hesitation".
Count 2: LF closes to RF w/o weight | FDC | Toes of both feet | body turn to L | Sway(S) | cont to rise 5 and 6
As you bring feet together, rise on toes.
Different from leading Wing, in which, Man allows upper body to do extra rotation to allow Lady to pass from one side of body to another.
In this Cross Hesitation, Man's arms remain on his right side, you want Lady to stay in your right front.
Count 3: Position held | FDC | TH (RF) | -- | Sways(S) | lower e/o 6
Follower
Count 1: RF fwd | FLOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | -- 
This is "Open Impetus"
See Open Impetus to Wing Notes
Big step. Depending on Man's lead, may have big swooping action here.
Count 2: LF to side | BDC | T | 3/8 btwn 1-2 | Sway(R) | rise e/o 2
Allow feet to turn, delay head.
Step should be adequately large in order to keep balance , keep extending left side, then lift left side to turn head to the right at end of 2
Count 3: RF to side in PP having brushed to LF | Pointing Center, moving DC | TH | 3/8 btwn 2-3, body turns less | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 3
Keep shoulders/arms toward Man.
Lift the left side of hip to Man, which makes Lady feel like the shoulder line is tilted upward.
Extension: shoulder and chest - "rotate up" feel like high jump. Head: do not break the neck.
Count 1: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Pointing Center, moving DC | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 4
This is "Cross Hesitation"
Shape to left. stay with Man. Don't peel R shoulder off Man.
Count 2: RF to side | BLOD | T | 1/4 btwn 4-5 | Sway(L) | cont to rise on 5 and 6
Monitor Man's position.
Do not step big. Turn feet underneath the body.
Count 3: LF closes to RF | BDC | TH | 1/8 btwn 5-6 | Sway(L) | lower e/o 6

Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot
Waltz Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot
The Fallaway Reverse And Slip Pivot is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Count 1: LF fwd | FDC | HT | Start to turn L | - | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Count 2: RF back in Fallaway, R side leading | BDW, Moving down LOD | T | 1/4 between step 1-2 | -- | Up
Count 3: LF back in CBMP and Fallaway | BLOD | TH | 1/8 between 2-3, body turns less | -- | Up, Lower e/o 3
Count &: RF back, LF held in CBMP | To center, toe turned in, end facing LOD or DW | THT | 1/4 between 3-4, 1/4 or 1/8 on 4 (pivot) | -- | CBM | --
Follower
Count 1: RF back | BDC | TH | -- | -- | Rise e/o 1 NFR
Similar to Double Reverse Spin, keep head left the whole time to help with the turn. Legs move backward, but body should not feel dancing backward. body feels upward, leftward curve dancing into Man's R side.
Lower first. RF back, curve, toe turned in. Center can't fall back. Hips more forward and up to Man. Stay connected. Head goes even more left, stay outside.
Lower center rotates, upper center ("dial") sway only. shoulder does not fall back. 
Count 2: LF back in Fallaway, L side leading | BDC, moving down LOD | T | -- | -- | Up
Keep chest area very lifted to Man.
LF bigger step to match the first step, don't cut it short. 
Use head weight to L to help with turn. Keep head left throughout to help with turn, Head stretched more left. 
Curve. moving BDC. Suck tummy in, a little lift. Stay connected with Man.
Count 3: RF back in CBMP and Fallaway (small step), LF held in CBMP | BDC, end facing center | T | 5/8 to L on step 3 (Pivot) | -- | CBM | Up, lower e/o 3
It feels hip is more twisted to left. Do not close left hip. Pick up left side and open left hip, Man's right hip in middle of Lady's left hip and center.
Lady keeps center lifted to Man. Use tummy muscle, pull center. 
Count &: LF fwd in CBMP, RF held in CBMP | To center, end BLOD, or BDW | TH | 1/4 or 1/8 on 4 (pivot) | -- | CBM 
Follow Man, don't gap. More active, fwd and up, not necessarily bigger step. Active doesn't mean moving all the time.
Note
Timing: 1 2 3& (1 1 1/2 1/2) OR, 1, 2 &, 3 (1 1/2 1/2 1)

Leader
Count 1: RF back R side leading | Down LOD | T | - | Sway (L or R) | - | Start to rise e/o 1
Sway: LLSS or RRSS
Count &: LF crosses loosely in front of RF | Facing center | T | 1/4 to R btwn 1-2 | Sway (L or R) | - | Continue to rise 2 and 3

Count 1: LF fwd L side leading | Down LOD | T | - | Sway (R or L) | - | Start to rise e/o 1
Lady will not brush on preceding Natural Spin Turn, but brush towards when moving into step 4 of Turning Lock to R.
Sway: either RRSS, or LLSS
Lady's head weight to left which helps the turn. Only turn to R going into count 3.
Count &: RF crosses loosely behind LF | Backing center | T | 1/4 to R btwn 1-2 | Sway (R or L) | - | Continue to rise on 2 and 3
Small step. hip fwd
Count 2: LF to side and slightly back | BDC | T | 1/8 btwn 2-3 | Sway(S) | - | -
LF sort of around Man, bigger step, so Lady can stay behind Man. Stretch. After weight is on LF, articulate head to PP.

Dance Central - Left Whisk
Waltz Left Whisk
Waltz Left Whisk is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Count 1: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | FDW, moving along LOD | H | - | - | - 
Count 2: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | TH | Body start to turn L | - | - | -
Count 3: RF crosses behind LF | FDW | T | Body turn to L | - | - | -
Man looks towards Lady's face, not over her R shoulder.
Man's RH will lower as the next step is taken.
Follower
Count 1: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Facing DC, moving along LOD | HT | start to turn L | - | CBM | -
Count 2: RF to side and slightly back | BDW | TH | 1/4 btwn 1-2 | - | -
RF forward, shape to R and close with Man, then R hip glued to Man. Follow hip curve.
Lady tends to have a tendency to take her R elbow in, keep elbow slightly out to maintain volume, extend her R arm (give to Man, create room to create shape)
Count 3: LF back in CBMP | BLOD | TH | 1/8 btwn 2-3 | - | -
To produce big shape:
Take step LF back (along original travel direction, don't go around Man), move weight back to LF (this is a step, otherwise you'll block Man), collect RF (crossed in front of LF, w/o weight).
Butt and hip fwd to Man, don't turn out of Man. Hips stop turning, but body can continue to turn more to shape/sway without changing base. 
Contra within own body. Both L and R rib cage and hips fwd to Man and shape to L (w/o turning hips more), R side of body is longer (very stretched). Don't "sit" bottom down, heavy. Pick up center and bottom. Really pick up L side ribs to Man while rotating body to L and extending head to L.
Head turns well to left and extend (without body peeling from Man) to finish the extension. Breathe out to extend head even more.
Keep arm/elbow forward, extend the arm fwd to Man (check R elbow angle), give it to Man. Don't break shoulder or peel away shoulder.
Note:
Can enter Left Whisk from different position, e.g. From Weave or Outside Change, ending in Closed Position, but with Lady's head turned to R.
No rise in Left Whisk.
Alternative Alignment - Man start with RF back, BLOD
Leader
Count 1: RF back | BLOD | TH | Start to turn L | - | CBM | -
Count 2: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | TH | 3/8 btwn steps 1-2, body turns less | - | - | -
Count 3: RF crosses behind LF | FDW | T | Body turn to L | - | - | -
Man looks towards Lady's face, not over her R shoulder.
Man's RH will lower as the next step is taken.
Follower
Count 1: LF fwd | FLOD | HT | start to turn L | - | CBM | -
Count 2: RF to side and slightly back | BDW | TH | 3/8 btwn 1-2 | - | -
Count 3: LF back in CBMP | BLOD | TH | 1/8 btwn 2-3 | - | -
Lady may overturn slightly to L on step 3 and turn her head well to the L.
Variation: Left Whisk on Count 1 with Contra Check
Man:
Dance Natural Spin Turn (123) and 1-4 of Turning Lock (1&2 3): end with: LF to side pointing DW
1: Cross RF behind LF into Left Whisk
2: Replace weight to LF
3: RF to side and slightly fwd pointing DW against LOD
1: LF fwd into Contra Check DW against LOD
Continue with steps 2-3 Contra Check turning 1/4 to L.
Lady:
Dance Natural Spin Turn (123) and 1-4 of Turning Lock (1&2 3): end with: RF to side pointing DC
1: turning L, cross LF behind RF into Left Whisk. Lady's head to Left. On ball of LF. The step is not too big (not as big as contra check). Weight in middle.
2: RF fwd. Head keep left.
3: LF side. Lady finish inline. (need to step enough around Man), head can keep left, or turn to right, make sure left side is very up. Chest is up to Man. Heel down.
1: RF back into Contra Check 
Continue with steps 2-3 Contra Check.

S: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Facing DW against LOD (Moving to Wall) | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 4
RF fwd in lane 1. Lady is in lane 2.
Q: LF to side | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 4-5 | Sway(S) | Up
Man is making lane change to the other side of the Lady (lane 3). Do not bring Lady across you, Man makes lane change.
Stretch left side.
Q: RF back R side leading | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 between 5-6 | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 6 
Compress right side, leading Lady into swivel action. Rotate through the hip, arms keep rotating across the body to right (arms are still in front of body, not behind.)
S: LF back in CBMP, RF crosses in front without weight. End in PP | DW Toe turned in. End facing DC | TH with pressure on T of RF | 1/4 turn to R on 7 | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Keep head poised through diagonal. Diagonal shape is important, do not take head weight back.
S: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Facing DC (moving to center) | HT | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 8
Q: LF diag fwd preparing to step OP L side leading | Facing DC | T | Sway(R) | Up
Use the leverage of R leg, swing left leg fwd ahead of body, closing the lady. Do not rotate Lady too far in upper body. Still keep an angle to come through for feather ending.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | Sway(R) | Up. Lower e/o 10
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | Sway(S) | CBM
Note -- When the RF starts to move to cross in front on step 7, either the Heel or Toe may be in contact with the floor. It will finish with the Toe in contact with the floor and the Heel only slightly raised with the R leg almost straight. 
The Weave from PP could follow step 7 in which case the aligment of the Weave from PP would be as in the Waltz Weave from PP. 
When the Outside Swivel is danced at the side of the room to be followed by Feather Ending into Top Spin, 1/8 turn to R is made on step 7 and 8-10 will be taken DC against LOD. Weave from PP may also be danced following this underturned Outside Swivel, Man using the Basic Weave alignment. If dancing this underturned Outside Swivel at a corner steps 8-11 will be taken DC of new LOD. Follow as for Feather.
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could be danced by checking OP on any Feather Movement taken DC and turning 1/4 to R. Follow --Silver--Feather Ending--Natural Turn (when danced as steps 4-6 above)--Natural Weave from PP--Gold--Curved Feather to Back Feather from PP--Natural Zig-Zag from PP
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could also be danced by checking OP on any Feather taken DW without turn and followed as in the previous paragraph.
Follower
S: RF back | Backing DC | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Don't open head too early here, wait till end of next step Q.
Q: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn) | Facing DW | HT | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | continue to rise on 2
Heel Turn is an abrupt, steep turn for Lady. 
When turning left, the right side is the anchor point, left side stretch. 
First part of turn is done through feet (base), feet turn more. Do not bring shoulders back too quickly.
Head is still L. Head slowly turns between end of this step and next Q. Don't open head too early, arrive on L leg first, wait for Man's lead to open head. Turn head slowly "milk it".
Q: RF diag fwd in PP | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 3
Second part is to finish the turn with upper body. 
Imagine always dancing forward.
Head turns to Promenade. 
This completes the "Open Telemark" part of the figure. "Natural Turn" part only applies to Leader. Lady's steps are not Lady's Natural Turn steps.
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Facing DW (moving to wall) | HT | -- | Sway(S) | Rise e/o 4
Very important for lady to finish behind Man on previous step. Then Lady's L leg should be behind Man's R leg and and should track Man's R leg movement forward, so it won't feel like Man is blocking Lady.
Allow Man to get ahead of you. 
Left shoulder connects to left hip. Keep shaping to left.
Allow arms to swing across the body.
Then dance straight forward, follow the Man with upper body, legs are tracking straight.
Q: RF fwd, between partner's feet | Facing DW | T | -- | Sway(S) | Up. 
Keep the shaping to left, allow R side body/shoulder go forward to Man. Swing R hip forward. Use standing leg to push, don't fall forward. 
Head position changes to left. Leave head behind, send middle of body forward, finish head with more extension. Keeping the wound-up shape for next step.
Movement is smooth and continuous, no stopping. 
One style is to keep slight R side lead through the Passing Natural. 
Q: LF fwd preparing to step OP L side leading | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 6
LF straight fwd. Relax into L knee/leg, head shaping leftward back, wind up for next swivel.
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP. LF closes slightly back, without weight. End in PP | Facing DW. End facing DC against LOD, (sometimes more turn is done, ending traveling DC) | HTH and pressure on IE of T of LF | 1/2 to R on 7 | Sway(S) | CBM | --
This is the swivel. Bigger step, keep shoulder parallel, turn hips and head. The swivel action is purely happening underneath the body, do not turn the shoulders.
Shoulder should end parallel with Man with hips turned out more.
Hips and and feet are in same alignment.
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Facing DC against LOD (moving to center) | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 8
Keep behind Man's hip.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing LOD | TH | 1/8 between 8-9 | Sway(L) | Up
Swing right side up and forward. Head back to left.
Feather finish through the base.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | TH | 1/8 between 9-10, body turns less | Sway(L) | Up. NFR. Lower e/o 10
S: RF back | Backing DC | T | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Can follow with Basic Weave.

The Foxtrot Natural Twist Turn is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Natural Twist Turn with Hover Feather Ending
Leader
S: RF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | Continue to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | - 
No upward swing (rise) on first 3 steps.
Q (1/2): LF to side | Backing DC | TH | 3/8 between steps 1-2 | Sway(R) | -
& (1/2): RF crosses behind LF slightly back | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(R) | -
Q: Twist on both feet. | Facing DC | Twist on T of RF and H of LF with feet flat. End with weight on RF | 3/8 on steps 4-5. Body turns slightly more. | Sway(S) 
S: End as in Heel Pull | Facing DC | T of RF with pressure on IE of T of LF | Sway(L) | Rise on 5. Body overturns on step 5.
Q: LF diag fwd preparing to step OP L side leading | Facing DC | T | - | Sway(L) 
Complete figure with a Hover Feather
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | - | Sway(S) 
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM 
Follower
S: LF back | Backing LOD | TH | Sway(S) | CBM | Continue to turn R
No upward swing (rise) on first 3 steps.
Head can keep left during the whole figure. Don't go back to straight position. Keep very left, more extended back during Weave.
Connect L shoulder down, Feel partner on the right side.
Styling option: start with head to L and by end of Q&Q, head shape to R.
Q (1/2): RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | Backing LOD | HT | Sway(L) | 1/2 between 1-2
Heel turn. Positive R side stretch. 
(Man is in an awkward leg position. Man will be stationary. Lady can't step too big.)
& (1/2): LF fwd, preparing to step OP, L side leading | Facing almost DW | T | Sway(L) | -
A series of T steps. Keep compact steps, soft relaxed knees and ankles. It's very cushioned actions. Even though it's on Toe, do not stand straight tall. 
R side connected to partner.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | T | Sway(S) | CBM | 1/8 between 2-4
Don't hesitate to place the RF. Continuous movement, no hold.
Head changed shape to R
S: LF to side, RF brushes to LF | Backing DC | T and IE of T of RF | Sway(R) | 1/4 between 4-5
Don't be hesitant to place the foot . First, place LF, finish the shape to R (L side very stretched, feel like sitting on L leg, compress, and then change shape) and 2nd part (&), change head shape to L (stretch R side) and compress into L knee.
RF will brush, before pushing off on L leg.
Head styling: starting with head shaped to R, when finishing the S, head is shaped to L.
Q: RF diag back, R side leading | BDC | TH | Sway(R)
This is a Hover Feather ending.
Q: LF back in CBMP | BDC | TH | Sway(S) 
S: RF back | BDC | T | Sway(S) | CBM
Natural Twist Turn with Natural Weave Ending
Leader
S: RF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | Continue to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | - 
No upward swing (rise) on first 3 steps.
Q (1/2): LF to side | Backing DC | TH | 3/8 between steps 1-2 | Sway(R) | -
& (1/2): RF crosses behind LF slightly back | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(R) | -
Q (and with next count S)
S: (With previous count Q) Twist on both feet. Ending with RF fwd and slightly to the side | Facing LOD 
No sway on S.
Q: LF to side and slightly back | Backing almost DC
QQQQQS: Continue with 3-8 of Natural Weave
Follower
S: LF back | Backing LOD | TH | Sway(S) | CBM | Continue to turn R
No upward swing (rise) on first 3 steps. Keep head extended very left during the twist.
Q (1/2): RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | Backing LOD | HT | Sway(L) | 1/2 between 1-2
& (1/2): LF fwd, preparing to step OP, L side leading | Facing almost DW | T | Sway(L) | -
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | T | Sway(S) | CBM | 1/8 between 2-4
S: LF to side and slightly back | Backing LOD | T | turning R
No Sway, LF swivels, knee and ankle relaxed. The foot ends in a slightly back position (after swiveling). 
Q: RF diag. fwd | Facing DC | T 
R shoulder goes down. Swinging side (L) is not the driving side, R side is driving side.
QQQQQS: Continue with 3-8 of Natural Weave | Finish BDW | T T T TH TH 
Head keep very left. Don't go back to "safe" straight position. Extend head even more left back during Weave. Send ribs to Man.
Note:
Lady optional styling: head could change to R during Weave. 
Natural Twist Turn with Closed Impetus And Feather Finish Ending
Leader
SQ&QQ: As for Natural Twist Turn 
Q: LF to side and slightly back | Backing DC against LOD
SQQS: RF back into Feather Finish
Follower
SQ&QQ: As for Natural Twist Turn
Last Q: T, RF brush, small step, then bring stomach muscle back, brush RF to LF before stepping out. 
Lady needs to monitor where Man's body is, where his center is.
Q: RF diag fwd having brushed to LF | Facing DC against LOD | TH
Need to have own individual balance.
L shoulder forward with RF fwd, opposite side of body is working with moving leg..
SQQS: LF fwd into Feather Finish | Ending BDC | HT TH TH 
Dissolve sway, use range of motion, taking head, spine to LF, Swing, turn feet underneath body.
Keep shoulders quiet. Arms belong to partner at all times (especially when going backward.)
Natural Twist Turn with Open Impetus Ending
Leader
SQ&QQ: As for Natural Twist Turn
Last Q: LF to side, but body is not traveling to side, flat. "side" is the finishing position. Think about stepping forward and finish to side.
Do not step too big. 
Q: LF diag fwd in PP L side leading | Pointing DC. body facing LOD | TH
Turn feet under waist, keep body with partner.
SQQS: RF fwd in PP and CBMP | Pointing DC. body facing LOD
Feather finish
Follower
SQ&QQ: As for Natural Twist Turn
Last Q: T, RF will brush LF
Q: RF to side in PP | Pointing to center, moving DC | TH
This is the Open Impetus.
SQQS: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Pointing to center, moving DC

Dance Central - Basic Weave
Foxtrot Basic Weave
This is a Bronze figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Q: LF fwd | DC Against LOD | HT | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at e/o 1
Q: RF to side | Backing LOD | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up 
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | T | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Up
The footwork of TH may be used. This will often result in a softer and more flowing movement. Rise on 4 will then be "Up, NFR"
Q: RF back | Backing DC | T | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | T | 1/4 between 4-5 | Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up, lower e/o 6 
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
Q: RF back | Backing DC Against LOD | TH | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly end of 1, NFR
Make sure belly button/lower center is constantly toward partner.
Q: LF to side | Pointing DC | T | 1/4 L between 1-2 | Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
If Man shapes, Lady turns her head to R
Leave head and only turn body to L, which effectively make head on the R side.
Keep head flat, knees will absorb foot rise. Do not pop up. 
During Weave, knees and thighs are ahead of body. Do not take head too fast and past feet. Relax R shoulder down. Take knees across standing leg when moving forward. 
Use leg to push, use butt muscle to move more, not sitting. Activate the muscle, but not like Samba tick, don't flat the hip crease. When using muscle to push and move more, it makes it easier to extend and shape upper body more too.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | T | -- | Sway(R) | Up
Traveling down LOD at this moment. Left shoulder is quite forward at this point.
Don't fall into the step, use L leg to push.
To create more volume, rotate hip to R, L shoulder more forward, shape diagonal forward (not down), extend head to create the shape. (Do not just bend back upper body to crate shape.)
Q: LF fwd | Facing DC | T | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Extended Weave: step 3 and 4 can be repeated many "QQ"s - side leading, lady project fwd left side. Don't peel off from Man. Center to Man. When Man starts to collect, that indicates finishing, but still Lady should do Toe leads in next step. Though written as "T", instructor says do TH to drive the next step.
Do not get into Man's space. -- very common error.
Keep left connected to Man, do not slide connection, do not step to side, step straight forward (there is CBM, but don't step "cross middle line".)
L shoulder/head/spine/hip connect down to LF so you don't get pulled backward.
Q: RF to side | Backing Wall | TH | 1/8 L between 4-5 | Sway(L) | Up
Swinging R leg, keep head left.
Point fwd, as transferring weight to RF turn foot and head to left. body turns less, leave upper body to Man to prepare next OP step.
Turn head back to left.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 L between 5-6 | Body turns less | Sway(L) | Up NFR Lower e/o 6
S: RF back | Backing DW | T | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Note:
More advanced Lady dancers may, if the wish, use ball of foot to whole foot on the step preceding this pattern.
Basic Weave can be used along LOD, but often used at corner.
When Man shapes, lady turn head as shown above.
The figure is flat, do not rise. Use knees to absorb foot rise.
Connecting with Reverse Turn:
After SQQ of Reverse Turn, ending Man BDW, do a check step on "S":
Man needs to step back with RF, shape to L, raising his L arm, small step
Lady could do either toe lead or heel lead, depending on what kind of effect Man wants to have
Man could collect first, then take back step, in which case, it would be heel lead for Lady.
If Man doesn't lower to collect, then Lady takes a toe lead.
Lady collecting LF to RF (toe dragging on floor) and then step fwd HT (or TH depending on Man), shape to L, raising R side, really extend L side, head extends to L as far as possible.
Then continue with Basic Weave.
Alignment:
Could be danced as shown above, 1-4 Reverse Turn will end BDW, movement is along LOD
OR, danced at corner to change LOD (more often used): 1-4 Reverse Turn to end BLOD near corner, check and Basic Weave turning one of the following to end DW of new LOD
1/8, 1/8, 3/8
1/4, 1/8, 3/4

Dance Central - Feather Step
Foxtrot Feather Step
This is a Newcomer figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd | LOD (*) | HT | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at e/o 1
There is CBM, so frame turns to R.
Step in between lady's legs.
Keep straight line through the whole Feather Step, no zig-zag motion.
Q: LF fwd, prepare to step OP L side leading | LOD | T | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | LOD | TH | Sway(R) | Up, lower e/o 3
OP: RF is on same track as LF for Man.
When it's outside partner, it sometimes feels like Man's left side is tilting when in fact it actually is straight.
Note the footwork is TH, don't do heel lead. The excruciatingly correct footwork is: point right toe forward, touching floor with outside edge of toe, and sliding that toe forward as you push off your left foot.
at the end, start unwinding as body lowers.
S: LF fwd | LOD | H | Sway(S) | CBM
Follower
S: LF back | BLOD (*) | TH | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at end of 1, NFR
Keep moving straight line through the whole Feather Step, no zig-zag motion. Lady only has body rise, no foot rise. LF steps back, slightly to left to give Man room. 
Because of CBM, frame turns to R (body and shoulder), focus on upper body rotating to R with vertical rotation to L (R side stretch longer)
Connect L shoulder to LF, as upper body rotates, hips will open up (rotate to R) in a 'passive way', take L hip toward Man
Gradual smooth movement, don't transfer weight too quickly to LF, which will gap with Man. 
Finish the step onto standing foot (LF). Left ribcage forward, head extends to left. Head is on the outside of the LF.
Connect smoothly with next step, important to keep extension/shape when moving RF back (do not stick butt out which changes pitch of the spine and reduces volume with Man). It might help to think creating even more shape and in fact the result would be keeping shape and not reducing space.
Q: RF back, R side leading | BLOD | TH | Sway(L) | up, NFR.
R hip is "swinging" back, RF is perfectly inline with moving direction, with R knee, not turned out.
Body and hip already turned at end of previous step. Dancing the body through a diagonal. Side leading, R shoulder and hip swing and stretch diagonally back. L shoulder forward. Body is very stretched diagonally. Body 3D stretched: outside partner rotated to R, vertical side sway, frontal curve.
Make sure connect middle left side of body to Man, stretch (really both sides are stretching). which will give bigger upper body shape as well (head left and forward). 
Keep the volume and space with Man. do not change the pitch of the spine (spine keeps moving backward).
While the spine is continuously moving, do not take upper body weight back too quickly with the foot. 
No foot rise on RF. Release toe of front foot as it starts to move back towards supporting foot. RF Drag L heel close to RF. Balance on RF with L toe on side of RF until Man places the next step, do not fall back.
This is a side leading back step (not a side step), hip is not turned more on this step, Do not turn out RF, RF is straight back step. Pay attention to foot position. 
Hip is constantly moving, not stopped. It can slow down, but not stopped. If R hip stopped, it will feel blocking Man.
Q: LF back CBMP, OP | BLOD | TH | Sway(L) | up, NFR, lower e/o 3
Lady needs to be perfectly balanced on RF and wait till Man places her LF. Lady does not step LF back on her own.
Do not step short, do not step before Man put his step down. Use stomach muscle to draw RF. 
Finish outside partner. Lady's left side should be extending quite forward, may feel twisted to R.
Controlled lowering into L knee, extend more, (not straighten up body, think about keeping skin in front of tummy stretched and not shortened/relaxed), absorb (do not rush into next step), release R leg faster, to prepare and give Man space to finish his leg (he will have space to bend his knee.)
Continue the movement (do not stop, smooth transition between figures), spine continues to move, keep the shape (do not straighten up upper body which reduces volume and space with Man), tummy stretched open. Arms breathe, give the arms from the back to Man.
Use the L leg/foot to push (into next step).
S: RF back | BLOD | T | Sway(S) | CBM
Body recovering from the twist to start squaring up with Man again.
Very easy to lose volume when stepping back. Keep shape.
Continuing into next step (e.g. Three Step) - make sure knee is top of right toe, straight knee, should not be turned out. 
Technique Notes
(*) Feather step can also be danced DW or DC.
Feather step: balance between 3&4 (or 7&8), L heel on the floor, not up too long, no foot rise, slide the R toe back. 
The feet are always passing, so there is less rise and fall than in waltz.
Practice Feather Step and Three Step continuously: 
Other than step 3 of Feather Step which is CBMP, all other steps are back in one straight tracks (leave room for Man). The CBMP step makes the movement veering toward mans' left (lady's right), but the movement should be straight. Don't make waves (either to left or to right). 
Pay attention to the coordination of shoulder/body horizontal and vertical rotation.
On the feather step, there is CBM on the first step, so the frame turns to the right. On the Three Step, CBM happens gradually across the three steps, so the frame is wound to the left by the end of the figure. The motion is like swinging like a monkey from vine to vine.
The feet stay in contact with the ground all the time. When stepping back, the leg needs to swing back like a pendulum. The tip of the toe touches first, and then one rolls back onto the foot. The heel does not touch the ground until the feet are even.
Feather finishes usually have a note "body turns less", but there is no note "body completes turn". Although there is slight turn to L on the second to last step of a Feather Finish, the body does not really complete turn until the last step.
Types of Feather
Bronze: Feather Step
Bronze: Feather Finish -- first step taken back -- see also Reverse Turn
Silver: Feather Finish started with Partner OP on L side
Silver: Feather Ending -- first step taken in PP
1st Q: Lady really swings her R hip/ribcage to close up with Man, R side ribs, extend head, keep arms forward (from the back shoulder), arm breathes fwd, Step RF under the shoulder.
2nd Q: Same as end of Feather Step, controlled lowering and release R leg to prepare. Push on LF (Lady)
Silver: Hover Feather
Silver: Feather Step started OP
Gold: Curved Feather
Gold: Back Feather

Foxtrot Change of Direction
This is a Bronze figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | H | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
At the preceding step (e.g. feather finish), frame should already be turning.
This step has CBM, with LF turned out a bit.
S: RF diag fwd R side leading, and close LF to RF slightly fwd without weight | DW R Toe pointing towards LOD, End facing DC | IE of TH, then IE of T of LF | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | --
At a corner, 3/8 or 1/2 turn may be made.
Pay attention to move along the same line/direction, do not change moving direction by doing step side - it's a fwd step. (though some coaches say this is a side step, don't do forward step.)
Big step, push off LF.
S: LF fwd in CBMP | Facing DC | H | Sway(S) | CBM | --
This is exactly like a preparation step, shaping to right
Follower
S: RF back | Backing DW | TH | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Head really turns to L, follow the L elbow. Collect LF and push off RF to side.
S: LF diag, back, L side leading, and close RF to LF slightly back without weight | Backing DW, End backing DC | IE of TH, then IE of T of RF | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | --.
Upper body stay with partner. do not get into his space.
Pull weight onto LF, with balance. Do not anticipate movement. Allow L hip to finish, then keep turning L. 
S: RF back in CBMP | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Option: This is like preparation step, shaping to left.
Depending on next, this could shape to R, head turn to R.

Dance Central - Closed Telemark
Waltz Closed Telemark
The Waltz Closed Telemark is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Count 1: LF fwd | FDC | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1
Use CBM and rise a bit early to lead heel turn.
Count 2: RF to side | almost BLOD | T | slightly less than 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up
Get strong rotation through the feet, do not come off RF too early, which will allow Lady to turn. A little hover helps to keep Lady in front of Man.
Count 3: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | TH | slightly over 3/8 between 2-3. body turns less | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 3
Count 1: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | DW | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM | -- 
Follower
Count 1: RF back | BDC | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Keep your own balance.
When starting the figure, feel left hip flexor working, do not think about R leg moving back, think about standing on the L leg to get weight moving into the foot.
Do not think about shoulder rotation at all, just think about strong diagonal stretch, stretching the left side,
Turn is all going to happen through base area. Lower center/hips are rotating. From sternum area, feel strong leftward, upward sway from sternum up, no rotation through shoulders. If you rotate shoulder, you'll end up on Man's left side.
Step straight back, not off to the side. Head: keep head at the same place, rotate body underneath and head ends up on the right and keep it there
Count 2: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn) | LOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | continue to rise on 2
Heel turn, the feeling is the turn is mostly done on RF, has a slight feeling of pushing off LF, then RF step back on 3 to finish.
Stay connected with Man.
Count 3: RF to side and slightly back | BDW | TH | 3/8 between 2-3, body turns less | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 3
Keep moving, don't hover and stop, strong, bigger step (Man is waiting, need more power). Keep head position (which will make it shape to right).
Keep head to right, then on 1 of next e.g Natural Spin Turn, rotate body underneath (no sharp head movement, just keep head at same place starting at 1, not later). 
Count 1: LF back in CBMP | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Next step, Man steps OP.

Closed Impetus And Feather Finish
Foxtrot Closed Impetus and Feather Finish
This is a Newcomer figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF back | BLOD | TH | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | - 
Step straight back, not to the side (which will it harder for Lady to catch up).
Q: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | FDC | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | rise e/o 2
Rise up balanced on step 2
Q: LF to side and slightly back | Backing DC Against LOD | TH | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 3
Push off foot on step 3. 
S : RF back | Backing DC Against LOD | THT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 4
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | PDC | T | 1/4 between 4-5, body turns less | Sway(R) | up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDC | TH | - | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 6
Correct footwork is: pointing right toe forward, touching floor with outside edge of toe, and sliding that toe forward as Man pushes off his left foot.
Do not do heel lead.
S: LF fwd | FDC | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM | -
Follower
S: RF fwd | FLOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | -
To get around Man (a lot of distance to cover), lower first, then step with CBM, take LF with it, but don't step LF too early.
Straighten the R leg (smoothly); do not bent R knee when R heel hit ground.
Q: LF to side | BDC | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | rise e/o 2
Step LF to side, late - till after body is turned. The whole motion should be a smooth motion.
Get onto LF first, before articulating head. Keep right side very forward to Man in PP. 
Collect center (belly button) to draw RF
Q: RF diag fwd having brushed to LF | Facing DC Against LOD | TH | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | up, lower e/o 3
Really push off LF. (body should be on Man's R side)
Head can optionally turn to R if Man does the shaping.
S: LF fwd | Facing DC Against LOD | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 4
Lower first, then step. Make sure upper body does not go into Man first and then shape back out (it's often unconsciously done.) Keep upper body shape.
Q: RF to side | BLOD | TH | 1/8 between 4-5 | Sway(L) | up
Don't take arm back. Arm "breathes", forward to Man. Head changes back to left (if it was previously turned to R.)
Q: LF back to CBMP | BDC | TH | 1/8 between 5-6 , body turns less | Sway(L) | up, NFR lower e/o 6
Left shoulder forward. 
S: RF back | BDC | T | Sway(S) | CBM

Dance Central - Reverse Turn
Foxtrot Reverse Turn (Feather Finish)
This is a Newcomer figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | FDC | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at e/o 1
Step straight forward DC.
Early rise to lead a heel turn, it's difficult for lady to do heel turn on bent knee, so man needs to rise enough to allow to have straight legs for heel turn. Man "walks with a particularly straight knee" which stops the movement forward and make Lady do heel turn.
Q: RF to side | BDW | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | up
The step is forward relative to the body. There is minimal pivot on the LF. The RF stepping forward relative to the body will thus be stepping between DC and center.The man then can pivot on the RF as he is transferring weight from the LF.
When leading heel turns, brush the foot, it helps to not fall over
Q: LF back | BLOD | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 3
Step straight back.
S: RF back | BLOD | THT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at e/o 4
Q: LF to side, slightly fwd | Pointing DW | T | 3/8 between 4-5, body turns less | Sway(R) | up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDW | TH | - | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 6
S: LF fwd | FDW | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM | -
Follower
S: RF back | BDC | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly 3/o 1, NFR
When feet are traveling backwards, mentally, think about body traveling forward, i.e. shoulder weight does not go back. 
Straight back. drive, big step (bigger steps actually helps with balance, if step is too small, body falls back more easily.)
Keep head even more left which helps to turn.
To initiate the turn, keep shoulders with partner, but turn hips to L, RF is slightly turned in when stepping (foot position always goes with hip position).
R shoulder down, L side stretched, strong diagonal. head follows the elbow.
Do not pop. Absorb rise with knees. Roll weight onto RF. L side positive.
Q: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn) | FLOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | up, NFR.
When dragging LF close to RF, be in sync with Man's leg movement, don't drag too quick.
Do not wait till last second to turn. Body is turning as LF is being dragged back.
Keep left side positive (i.e. body is turned 1/8 to R), do not over turn shoulder. Do not end square with Man. 
Do not pop. Absorb rise with knees.
Heel turn is not on straight knee, legs are slightly bent all the time.
Q: RF fwd | FLOD | TH | - | Sway(R) | up, NFR, lower e/o 3
Push LF off, don't fall short stepping RF forward. Slide ball of feet forward. Settle at the end. Next step starts with heel lead.
Because of the sway to R, L shoulder feels high (because of stretching of L side, but do not raise L shoulder itself.)
S: LF fwd | FLOD | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 4
Lower first, then step, bigger step, straight forward, don't go sideways. Do not turn out foot. Stay very left.
Q: RF to side | BW | TH | 1/4 between 4-5 | Sway(L) | up
Though written as "side step", it is really a swinging "fwd" step, then turn the feet and body. Lower heel, collect before stepping next step in CBMP.
Q: LF back in CBMP | BDW | TH | 1/8 between 5-6, body turns less | Sway(L) | up, NFR, lower e/o 6
Leave left shoulder forward, don't lead back.
S: RF back | BDW | T | - | Sway(S) | CBM | -
Continue with next step, do L toe release.
Note:
Step 4-7 of the Reverse Turn is Feather Finish and may be commenced in other alignments. Less turn may be made.
Feather Finish is the ending of many patterns.
See Heel Turn technique in Standard Technique on Turns.

Dance Central - Natural Turn
Foxtrot Natural Turn
This is a Newcomer figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd | LOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at e/o 1
Early rise to lead a heel turn, it's difficult for lady to do heel turn on bent knee, so man needs to rise enough to allow to have straight legs for heel turn.
Q: LF to side | BDC | T | 3/8 to R btwn 1-2 | Sway(R) | up 
It's really a forward step, transfer weight and then turn hips.
Q: RF back | BLOD | TH | 1/8 btwn 2-3 | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 3
After turning feet and hips to face may partner on step 2, hips and shoulders stay flat, as lady steps between leader's feet.
S: LF back | BLOD | TH | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | -
S: RF to side, small step (Heel Pull) | FDC | H, IE of foot, WF, IE of LF | 3/8 btwn 4-5 | Sway(L) | -
Turn less at corner to face new LOD or DC of new LOD. See note.
S: LF fwd | FDC | H | body turn to L | Sway(S) | CBM | -
Note:
The figure can start Man facing DW, in which case, the first step will turn 1/4 between 1-2 and 1/8 between 2-3.
The figure can also be used at corner, in which case, Man turn 1/4 between 4-5 to face new LOD, or 1/8 to face DC of new LOD.
Follower
S: LF back | BLOD | TH | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at end of 1, NFR
Connect L shoulder down, R side positive. Do not move upper body too quickly off the feet. Allow lower center to rotate. 
In Foxtrot, this first step has less of a curve than Waltz Natural Turn (which is not a heel turn), it is fairly straight back step with a very slightly curve.
Roll weight onto LF.
Q: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | LOD | HT | 1/2 btwn 1-2 | Sway(L) | up, NFR.
Heel turn is not on straight knee, legs are slightly bent all the time.
At end of the step, shoulder should not be square, R side positive (fwd) - body is rotated to 1/8 L (i.e. don't over turn in previous heel turn). Wind up.
Q: LF fwd | LOD | TH | - | Sway(L) | up, NFR, lower e/o 3
Really push RF to get LF fwd further, don't fall short.
When LF is step forward, body is still diagonal, R side positive. 
Then dissolve the sway when coming down to 2 feet together.
Hips and frame are square to LOD (different from Natural Weave).
S: RF fwd | LOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | -
Be careful, this is inline with Man, not outside partner as in Natural Weave. Lower first and then step forward.
S: LF to side | BDC | TH, IE of T of RF | 3/8 to R btwn 4-5 | Sway(R) | -
Continue moving center to LF, do not leave weight in between feet. 
Then continue moving L hip back to prepare for next step
S: RF back, having brushed to LF | BDC | T | body turn to L | Sway(S) | CBM | -
Difference between Waltz and Foxtrot:
In foxtrot, we only drive on the first step, and swing the leg on the second step. In waltz, we drive on the first and second step, and the swing is what brings our feet together to settle at the end of the third step.
In Waltz Natural Turn, step 1 is a driving step, then, Man puts his left foot forward on the ground, without weight, pointing it, and then Man push off the right foot. Man is turning around lady's outside shoulder. (Both steps are driving steps.)
In this Foxtrot Natural Turn, after the first step, Mans turns his body, bringing his left foot under his body, before swinging the RF foot back. This is part of the difference between leading the lady to pass her feet or do a heel turn, respectively. Man is turning around lady's sternum.
The Foxtrot Natural Turn, Natural Weave, and Hover Cross have the same steps for the first two steps, and it is after that they differentiate. For the leader,
Step 1: RF forward, with CBM
Step 2: LF forward, and then when transferring weight, turn hips. Brush RF to LF before taking next step on RF.

Open Telemark and Feather Ending
Foxtrot Open Telemark and Feather Ending
The Open Telemark and Feather Ending is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | FDC | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1 
Compress down through right shoulder, hip and foot. Use CBM to send lady to inside turn. Rise through LF leading heel turn. Then, let right hip swing ahead. 
Q: RF to side | Backing DW | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up
When leading to PP ending, Man left his LF there (like in spiral turn), there is no brush, LF then steps out in step 3 to PP.
Base rotate to left more than PP
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd in PP | Pointing DW | Body Facing Wall | TH | 1/2 between 2-3. body turns less | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 3
Body turn less to PP. Keep body facing Lady.
S: RF fwd in PP and CBMP | Pointing DW | Body Facing Wall | HT | -- | Sway(S) | Rise e/o 4
After stepping on RF, anchor RF, allow L leg to swing ahead of body, create diagonal stretch. 
Q: LF diag fwd preparing to step OP | Facing DW | T | Sway(R) | Up
Upper body rotate slightly left, indicating Lady to close her head and do Feather Finish
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | TH | Sway(R) | Up, lower e/o 6
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | H | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
S: RF back | BDC | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Left turning figures - anchor right shoulder and hip down, turn the base. keep upward leftward diagonal stretch. Do not turn L shoulder back.
Do not turn head to PP on this count, it's too early.
Q: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn) | LOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | continue to rise on 2
Do not lock the knees, keep knees flexed, keep action soft. Take stomach muscle back.
Allow arms and upper body to continue following the Man, upper body will finish the turn.
Head started turning to PP into next step.
Q: RF diag fwd in PP, R side leading | Pointing to LOD | TH | slight body turn to L | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 3
Body turn more to PP.
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Pointing to LOD (Moving DW) | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 4
It's different to do feather finish from PP. Lady really has to use this step 4 (S) and next step 5 (Q) to move a lot of space to keep up with Man.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing Wall | TH | 1/4 between 4-5 | Sway(L) | up
Swing R hip forward, then allow feet to turn under the body.
Body never feels travel side ways. Body feels like traveling forward.
R side stretch diagonally.
Keep arms forward in front of body.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 between 5-6, body turns less | Sway(L) | up, NFR Lower e/o6
S: RF back | Backing DW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --

Dance Central - Top Spin
Foxtrot Top Spin
The Top Spin is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing Against LOD | T | 1/8 to L between preceding step and 1 | Sway(S) | Up
Preceding step is generally a Feather Finish. To continue with Top Spin (to avoid traffic for example), the previous Feather Finish is cut short so you can respond quickly into Top Spin action. 
"Backing Against LOD" means Man is facing LOD but your next step will be stepping backward (against LOD). This is different from BLOD, which is "Backing LOD" your next step is down LOD.
Start turning left hip to left. This is not a large step. Then use CBM. 
Q: RF back | Backing DW Against LOD | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | CBM | UP
Connect right shoulder down to right side.
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DC of new LOD | T | 1/4 between 2-3. body turns less | Sway(R) | UP
Swing left hip forward.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Lower e/o 4
See Note for other alignments. Could finish DW of new LOD.
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
Q: RF fwd in CBMP OP | Facing Against LOD | T | 1/8 to L between preceding step and 1 | Sway(S) | Up
When Man cut short Feather Finish, Lady respond to keep weight on T (at end of Feather Finish).
Follow Man's body, do not be too powerful, the figure is compact. Mainly used in corners or get out of trouble. Man can change alignments. 
"Facing Against LOD": think of typical Feather Finish ending, you'll be changing direction, so you'll be stepping forward (against LOD).
Q: LF fwd | Facing DW Against LOD | T | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Monitor through Man's central line, keep arm light.
Connect head, shoulder hip to left foot.
Q: RF to side | Backing new LOD | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Up
Allow feet to swivel, feet turns more than body
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | TH | 1/8 between 3-4, body turns less | Sway(L) | Up. NFR Lower e/o 4
Think about stretching diagonally to the right, rather than rotation in upper body.
S: RF back | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Notes
On step 1 the footwork of TH may be used. This will often result in a softer and more flowing movement. When this footwork is used the rise will be "Up NFR" on step 2. 
On step 6 of the preceding Reverse Turn with Feather Ending, Man's body will be facing DW and the Lady will be backing DW.
Silver and Gold: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish may be danced turning 1/2 on step 1-3 to back against LOD and 3/8 on Feather Finish to face DC against LOD. Continue with Top Spin to face DW.
Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel may be danced, turning only 1/8 to R for Man, 3/8 for Lady on step 7 to follow with Feather Ending DC against LOD into Top Spin.

Q: RF to side | Pointing DW of new LOD | T | 1/2 between 2-3. Body turns less | Sway(S) | Up
Dissolve sway and changing sway direction
Base (feet) swivel more than body. (3 different alignment points)
Allow body to slide to the other side.
Contact point is light, brushing past partner.
Q: LF fwd in CBMP, OP, on L side | Facing DW of new LOD | T | slight body turn to R | Sway(L) | Up
Q: Transfer weight back to RF in CBMP | Backing DC against LOD | T | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Q : LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DC | T | 1/4 turn between 5-6. Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
Body slides back to basic position.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up. Lower e/o 7

Follower
S: LF back | Backing DW | TH | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Q: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | Facing LOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | continue to rise on 2
Do not lock the knees, keep knees soft, compressed.
Multiple Q's, keep flat, don't rise
Q: LF to side | Backing new LOD | T | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up
Think about taking the step fwd (not side).
Keep body light, think about "up and over" Man's arm.
Keep own internal balance. Flat.
Q: RF back in CBMP | Backing DW of new LOD | T | 1/8 between 3-4. Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
Let hip turn to R. Rise on L side/hip. Shoulder and head really doesn't change position.
Lady keeps her head to left (when first learning this figure), stretch left side. Alternatively, Lady can shape head to right. Slightly lift left side.
Shaping head to R goes with the sway, end of previous step 3. Make head movement really smooth, do not get to shaping right too fast
Keep head to right on steps 4-5, extend upper body even more on step 4, then return to normal starting end of step 5 (Q), then step 6 (Q).
It's all lower body. Do not turn shoulder, do not pull shoulder away from Man.
Lady slides from Man's R side to his L side. It's L side to L side connection with partner.
Q : Transfer weight fwd to LF in CBMP, OP, on Man's L side | Facing DC against LOD | T | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up. NFR. Lower e/o 5
Keep head to R
Isolate body, turn purely in feet underneath body. Allow arms to stay as still as possible.
Q: RF to side | Backing LOD | TH | 1/8 between 5-6 | Sway(L) | --
Lady slide back to Man's R side.
Head returns to normal position.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | TH | 1/8 between 6-7. Body turns less | Sway(L) 
S: RF back | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM

Foxtrot Natural Weave
This is a Bronze figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd | LOD | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | rise at e/o 1
Q: LF to side | Backing almost DC | T | Just under 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Up
The continuance of the turn between 2-3 is very important. Less turn can be made between 1-2.
It's really a forward step, then transfer weight and turn shoulders and ribs to right, to lead lady outside partner.
Q: RF back, R side leading | Backing DC | T | Slight turn to R between 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(L) | Up
TH may be used on this step 4. the rise will be "Up NFR on step 5"
Q: RF back | Backing DC | T | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | T | 1/4 between 5-6, Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up, lower e/o 7
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
S: LF back | BLOD | TH | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly end of 1, NFR
At the end of S, pay special attention not to drop left shoulder. Keep it very "up".
Scooping action, keep head movement smooth.
Q: RF close to LF (Heel Turn) | DC | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | cont to rise on 2
If Man shapes, Lady turns her head to R. (can keep head left. But if Lady turns her head to R, make sure she doesn't go into his space. Lady still needs to stay left. No crooked neck.)
Though it says "cont to rise on 2", Foxtrot, in general, is very flat. Often, Lady tends to pop up to much. Suppress the rise, stay fairly flat. Absorb rise with knee
Connect R shoulder down, grounded, drive through R side. 
Compared to normal Natural Turn, this heel turn in Natural Weave has less turn, but body kept rotate to R, it becomes L side positive to prepare for OP. 
Raise the ankles, feet goes onto toes. It feels like ankles/knees/thighs/hips are quite forward connected with Man, increases front curve in head as well. Create counter balance with knees forward and head back, so Lady can balance on her own.
L side moving forward (feels like L shoulder forward)
Q: LF fwd, preparing to step OP, L side leading | DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | Up
Do not pop up, stay flat, absorb rise with knee.
Think about extend right side even more, do not drop left side.
Shaping: head could turn right, but keep head weight left. Do not get into Man's space.
Different from Natural Turn: hips stay square, but frame continues to turn to do left side leading so lady can step OP on step 4 in Natural Weave.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | DC | T | -- | Sway(R) | Up
Shaping: head right
Use CBMP so Lady can pass Man.
Flat (Whenever there are multiple's of Qs in Quickstep, rise is suppressed, stay flat)
Q: LF fwd | DC | T | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Shaping: head right
Head/spine all on left side of body, connect down to LF. 
The weave part can be repeated. 
Some prefer the last weave to have the rise (signaling the end of weave) on LF
Q: RF to side | Backing Wall | TH | 1/8 between 5-6 | Sway(L) | Up
Swing R side/ R leg. RF point fwd first, then turn feet. Head will turn to L as well.
Shaping: head turn back to left on this step.
More advanced way of doing it is to swing R leg, RF steps forward down LOD and then swivel, ending BDW.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 between 6-7, Body turns less | Sway(L) | Up. NFR Lower e/o 7
L side forward.
S: RF back | Backing DW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Could directly follow Change of Direction.
Note:
The way to tell what step Man is leading Lady into:
Natural Turn: Man does not shape, Man does not turn.
Natural Weave, Man shapes body, leads Lady to turn their head to R. Man does not turn body to his R.
Hover Cross: Man shapes and turns.
The Weave can go more toward center or down the floor, depending on how close the couple is to the center.
Silver and Gold: The Natural Weave may be started in PP in which case the Lady would omit her Heel Turn and step diagonally forward RF on step 2. Precedes would be: Open Telemark, Hover Telemark to PP, Outside Swivel, Open Impetus

Dance Central - Hover Telemark
Foxtrot Hover Telemark
The Hover Telemark is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | HT | Start to turn L | Swau(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 1
Compress right shoulder (hip and foot), use CBM sending Lady to inside of turn, stretch abdominal muscle through strong diagonal to the left
Q: RF to side LF brushes towards RF | Facing LOD | T (RF), then IE of T of LF | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Continue to rise on 2
Swing motion.
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DC | TH | 1/8 between 2-3. body turns less | Sways(S) | Up. Lower e/o 3
When landing on LF, small range of motion to recover from sway.
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Note unusual type of rise
Follower
S: RF back | Backing DW | TH | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 1 NFR
Small CBM through the hip allowing L hip to release back. Step RF back, anchor right shoulder. R hip is moving across inside of right foot which will allow the hip to swing. Body is picked up through strong diagonal.
Q: LF to side, RF brushes towards LF | Backing LOD | T (LF) then IE of T of RF | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Continue to rise on 2
When traveling backwards, never turn shoulders so you don't cross center line. When going back, upper body is not a rotary motion, body should be stretching diagonal.
Left side is very lengthened, stretched. Head shaped to right. Keep elbows in front of body. Pick up stomach back to spine.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing DC | TH | 1/8 between 2-3, body turns less | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 3
Then dissolve the sway.
S: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Note:
Hover Telemark can finish in Promenade Position. See Hover Telemark to PP.

Dance Central - Weave From PP
Foxtrot Weave from Promenade Position
The Weave from Promenade Position is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd in PP and CBMP | Pointing DC Body facing LOD | HT | Sway(S) | rise e/o 1
feet, low center, high center: the offset position of these 3 points must be maintained through PP and weave. 
Q: LF fwd | Facing DC | T | start to turn L | Sways(S) | CBM | Up
Angle left shoulder and hip down, do not pull weight back.
Step through a curved line.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing DW | T | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Up
Energy still moves diagonal center. Do not think about BLOD.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 3-4 | Sway(L) | Up
Pull center back
Q: RF back | Backing LOD | T | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Up
Connect R shoulder and hip down, Man is inside of turn.
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing DW | T | 3/8 between 5-6 Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up. Lower e/o 7
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | H | Sway(S) | CBM
Note -- On step 4 the Footwork of TH may be used. The Rise on 5 will then be "Up NFR".
Alternate alignments for the Weave from PP may be found in the Waltz charts.
Follower
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Pointing to center (moving DC) | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1
Keep center toward the Man.
Body doesn't turn till second step.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing DC | T | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | Up
Depends on how Man shapes. Head can keep left or right. Head following the arc.
If shaping to R, keep head position, turn body underneath (base rotate), effectively, head will shape to right during weave. 
Think step forward and it will end in "side and slightly back"
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing to LOD | T | 3/8 between 2-3 Body turns less | Sway(R) | Up
Keep knee soft, do not stick tummy to man, pull center back.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing LOD | T | -- | Sway(R) | Up
Q : LF fwd | Facing LOD | T | start to turn L | Sways(S) | CBM | Up. 
R side is the driving side, left side is swinging fwd. The driving side is always the lower side, not the swinging side.
Q: RF to side | Backing Wall | TH | 1/4 between 5-6 | Sway(L) | Up
Closing head to left.
Swivel the feet. Feet turns more.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 between 6-7 Body turns less | Sway(L) | Up. NFR Lower e/o 7
S: RF back | Backing DW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --

Dance Central - Reverse Wave
Foxtrot Reverse Wave
The Reverse Wave is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1 
Rise at the end.
Q: RF to side | Backing Wall | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up
Q: LF back | Backing DW | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Up. Lower e/o 3
Not a very big step. Slightly different step from normal Reverse Turn because of the following figure (Reverse Wave).
Sway and receive the weight. Next step will be providing the drive.
S: RF back | Backing DW | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Driving step.
Q: LF back | Curving towards LOD | T | 1/8 between 4-6 | Sway(R) | Rise e/o 5
Q : RF back | Backing LOD | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up. Lower e/o 6
S: LF back | Backing LOD | TH | start to turn to R | Sway(S) | CBM | --
S: RF to side small step (Heel Pull) | Facing DC | H, IE of foot, whole foot then IE of LF | 3/8 between 7-8 | Sway(L) | --
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | Body turn to L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Special Note -- Although the rise is taken from the T of LF on step 5, when the RF starts to move back for step 6 the R Heel must be in contact with the floor.
Note -- There are five alignments of the Reverse Wave
Facing LOD 3/8 between 1-3, 1/8 between 4-6, 3/8 between 7-8 (or less at corner)
Facing LOD 3/8 between 1-3, 1/4 between 4-6 to back DW of new LOD, 1/4 between 7-8
Facing DW 1/2 between 1-3, then continue as in alignment (1)
Facing DW 1/2 between 1-3, then continue as in alignment (2)
Facing DC 3/8 between 1-3, 1/4 between 4-6 to back the new LOD, 3/8 between 7-8.
Alignments 2,4, and 5 are used to turn a corner.
Follower
S: RF back | Backing LOD | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
RF back, then keep head there.
Anchor R shoulder down. L side positive. 
Q: LF closes to RF (Heel Turn) | Facing DW | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | continue to rise on 2
Lady heel turn, keep head, turning body underneath, effectively head is now shaped to right. Extend.
Alternatively, the head can keep left/back at previous S, when switching weight to LF at this Q, head draws big arc and change to R. (Action started from foot, ankle, hip to head) (delaying head changes)
Leave shoulder still turned to R, not flat with Man at this time. Like winding up for next move.
Q: RF fwd | Facing DW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up. Lower e/o 3
Slide fwd more. 
Keep the shape to the R, head is to R. Shoulder rotated to R. L hip fwd/faster.
Enjoy this moment "longer", don't rush changing head to L.
S: LF fwd | Facing DW (moving to wall) | H | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Wave part: Use R leg, range of motion, curve with Man, stay with his right side. slowly turning head back smoothly.
LF fwd, Really push L hip diagonally forward (but not locked), side leading. Head changed to shape to L. 
After LF steps fwd, L shoulder connected to LF. change R shoulder forward at this time.
Q : RF fwd | Curving towards LOD | HT | 1/8 between 4-6 | Sway(L) | Rise e/o 5
Heel lead. Really swing hip to R, to Man, use L leg under butt muscle.
R side body ribs to Man. Head extends more. 
Produce a curve starting from foot, leg and body, coming out from head. Use R leg, bring L leg underneath body. Head curve leftwards. Drive with the leg, not upper body.
Head starting changing to L smoothly. Think about head taking more "space" explore the space. Enjoy the moment longer.
Q: LF fwd | Facing LOD | TH | -- | Sway(L) | Up. Lower e/o 6
Head to L.
S: RF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | --
2nd part of Natural Turn finish. Could do Closed Impetus or Open Impetus here. 
S: LF to side | Backing DC | TH, then IE of T of RF | 3/8 between 7-8 | Sway(R) | --
S: RF back having brushed to LF | Backing DC | T | Body turn to L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Extended Reverse Wave:
Reverse Wave can be extended. Add the following steps SQQ after step 6:
Man: continue BLOD
S: TH, LF back, 
Q: T, RF back, R shoulder leading
Q: TH, LF back in CBMP
Lady: continue facing LOD. Poise is not back weight, but shoulder is dancing in the back of the feet, do not fall forward. 
S: HT, RF fwd, then relax R shoulder down, allow left thigh and knee to dance forward, ahead of body. Drive out of R hip. Swing L hip up and forward for next step
Q: T, LF fwd, L shoulder leading, prepare to step OP. 
Q: TH, RF fwd, CBMP, OP. Pull RF underneath the body. Body is shaping back, which is because center of body is going forward, not bending backward. 
then continue from step 4 again. 
Head: can keep head left, or shape to R. The feeling is legs are dancing ahead of body.

Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending
Foxtrot Fallaway with Weave Ending
The Foxtrot Fallaway with Weave Ending is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | HT | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
&: RF back in Fallaway, R side leading | Backing DW, moving down LOD | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 2
Slight flexing of the knees; heel lowers towards floor and may lightly touch
Q: LF back in CBMP and Fallaway | Backing DW, moving down LOD | T | No turn | Sway(S)| Down. Rise e/o 3
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing DW | T | - | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 4
Slight flexing of the knees; heel lowers towards floor and may lightly touch
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing LOD | T | 1/8 between 4-5 | Sway(S) | | Down. Rise e/o 5
QQQS: 4-7 of overturned Basic Weave | T T TH T | Sway(SRRS) | CBM on step 6 and 9
Follower
S: RF back | Backing DC | THT | - | Sway(S) | Rise e/o 1
Lady's head is to left at this time. Keep previous head shape (maybe think about doing more).
THT footwork: There is a bounce/rise action. dancing the last T footwork is to dance body forward to get onto Toe of RF. It creates lightness through the legs.
Release L leg faster.
&: LF back in Fallaway, L side leading | Backing DC, moving down LOD | T | - | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 2
LF bigger step, more like a side step LOD. follow Man's hips. Weight onto L leg. Don't over turn shoulders or turn shoulders early. Shoulder is flat, going into Fallaway.
Stay very left, don't get in front of Man. 
It feels like Man stays in the fallaway longer (compared to Fallaway and Slip Pivot).
Default is a reverse weave action, if leader wants to do fallaway, he will block at the hips, which makes lady open immediately and do a Fallaway.
Turn head into PP position. Keep volume/space with Man (think about even more shape).
Use glute hip more forward to Man, shoulder toward Man (helps to create the PP shape), do not open up side.
Q: RF back in CBMP and Fallaway | Backing DC, moving down LOD | T | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Down. Rise e/o 3
Keep head to right. 
Keep body to Man, very stretched. Keep head volume with Man.
Cushion the knees
Q: LF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing to LOD | T | 3/8 between 3-4, body turns less | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 4
Swivel RF, not turning body and then step LF fwd. L side positive. Keep shoulder parallel to Man.
Feels like left shoulder is leading and stretching forward
The energy has to keep very leftward (stretch L side) and not get into Man's space (when head is to R, easy to get into Man's space)
Head options:
can keep head to R all the way till feather finish. Don't rush turning head, smooth movement. stretch to L.
Or at this time, take the time to turn head back to left, articulate the head.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing LOD | T | Slight body turn | Sway(S) | | Down. Rise e/o 5
Body diagonally stretched.
QQQS: 4-7 overturned Basic Weave | T TH TH T | | Sway(SLLS) | CBM on step 6 and 9
QQ: swing R side, and keep side leading position. Do not finish square and flat to Man. 
Changing angle from right to left, spine and head to left
Head turns back to left when RF to side/back closing up with Man.
Release leg at very end to give room to Man to step.

Dance Central - Natural Telemark
Foxtrot Natural Telemark
The Natural Telemark is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd | Facing DW | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1
Leading heel turn, stretch body through diagonal. The sway is less than other figures. There is more rotation in this figure.
Q: LF to side | Backing DC | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Up
Continue turning through the feet.
Q: RF side (small step) | Facing DC | T | 1/2 between 2-3. | Sway(S) | Up
Compress into knee without lowering into heel.
Continue through diagonal.
Q: LF diag fwd, preparing to step OP, L side leading | Facing DC | T | -- | Sway(L) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 5
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Note -- 1/2 or 5/8 turn may be used at a corner. Amount of turn in each case will be 1/4 between 1/2, whether started facing LOD or DW
Follower
S: LF back | Backing DW | TH | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Heel Turn to Right, access point is through left shoulder. Body has strong diagonal, feel body is going forward, don't think about bending upper body backward.
Q: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | Facing LOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | continue to rise on 2
Q: LF to side, RF brushes toward LF | Backing DC | T | 3/8 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up
Take head and weight to LF, compress L knee, then brush RF.
This step stays on T, there is no toe release for LF into next step.
Q: RF diag back R side leading | Backing DC | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up
Bring LF underneath body, skimming floor, then step back.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | TH | -- | Sway(S) | Up. NFR. Lower e/o 5
There is RF toe release when LF is stepping back on this step.
S: RF back | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Note -- When underturned, the Lady will always turn 3/8 between 1-2
